attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Substep protocol formalization + jj revsets cheatsheet (0.42.0-4.5) [73]
- init-clone-refactor recovery + post-mortem playbook (0.42.0-4.6) [74]

**Project:** `vc-x1` — Rust CLI managing dual-repo (app + `.claude` session) workflows over jj/git. Version `0.42.0-4.6`, mid multi-step cycle.

**Repo state:** both repos clean at `main` (`00739393` app / `7733a512` .claude), commit `docs: init-clone-refactor recovery WIP (0.42.0-4.6)`. App `@` is empty; `.claude` `@` has the usual live session writes.

- -0…-3 done (plan, scope.rs enum, init/sync retrofits)
- **-4 current** (push --scope, state-machine becomes scope-aware) — last commit was the `-4.6` recovery / WIP marker
- -5…-7 + close-out pending

**Top of todo (per `notes/todo.md:8-27`):** `-4.6` was committed mid-flow; one drafted item still needs to land — a top-of-Todo entry "Finish `init-clone-refactor` cycle" (DRY init+clone, consolidate `-6.x` substep ladder, ship 0.41.1 before `--scope` rolls further). content.content:
1	# Chores-07.md
2	
3	General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
4	post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
5	07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
6	0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
7	boundary.
8	
9	Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
10	when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
11	Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
12	implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.
13	
14	## init + clone redesign (0.41.1)
15	
16	Empirical validation 2026-04-27 against `vc-x1 0.42.0-3` (see
17	`notes/vc-x1-init.md`) surfaced cosmetic anomalies and a
18	substantive design gap. Init's flag surface (`--repo-local`,
19	`--repo-remote`, `--owner`, `--dir`, `[NAME]`) carries 6+
20	mutually-exclusive preflight checks just to prevent impossible
21	combinations from being typed. Clone already implements a
22	unified-positional alternative (URL / `owner/name` shorthand);
23	this cycle extends that pattern to init, adds POR support to
24	both, and unifies their CLI surfaces.
25	
26	Branched off 0.41.0 (commit `6747a27`). On close-out, the
27	in-flight 0.42.0 work rebases on top.
28	
29	### Command structure
30	
31	```
32	vc-x1 init  <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por] [--private] [--dry-run]
33	vc-x1 clone <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por]             [--dry-run]
34	```
35	
36	Identical surfaces modulo `--private` (init only).
37	
38	`<TARGET>` accepts:
39	
40	- **URL** — `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
41	  (detected by `://` or SSH `<host>:<path>` shape with `@`).
42	- **`owner/name` shorthand** — single `/`, no path prefix;
43	  resolved to `git@github.com:owner/name.git`.
44	- **Path-prefixed** — `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`. Path
45	  IS the target; last component is the workspace name. Bare
46	  `.` and bare `NAME` are errors (require explicit prefix).
47	
48	`[NAME]`:
49	
50	- For URL / `owner/name` forms: overrides the derived
51	  destination dir name. `vc-x1 clone owner/foo my-name`
52	  clones into `./my-name/` instead of `./foo/`.
53	- For path-prefix form: error if combined (path already
54	  specifies the full target).
55	- Path-form for clone: error (clone needs a remote URL;
56	  see "Operations" for clone's target rules).
57	
58	`--scope`:
59	
60	- `code,bot` (default): both repos, dual-repo layout.
61	- `por`: single repo, no `.vc-config.toml` written.
62	- `code` / `bot` standalone: error. Reason — these are
63	  config-lookup keywords; init has no config to look up
64	  against, and clone's simplification omits them too. The
65	  manual decomposition (clone code as POR, clone bot as
66	  POR, place at `.claude/`, run `vc-x1 symlink`) covers
67	  the rare cases where a user wants the dual layout
68	  composed by hand.
69	
70	`--private`: only meaningful for init when creating a new
71	GitHub repo. Sets visibility at `gh repo create`. Silently
72	warn-and-ignore if the remote already exists. Errors on
73	clone (clone never creates a remote).
74	
75	`--dry-run`: validate the operation as far as possible
76	without side effects. Print what would be done.
77	
78	### Operations
79	
80	**Init** is *smart* — it detects the target dir's state and
81	chooses the right operation.
82	
83	| Target state | `--scope=code,bot` | `--scope=por` |
84	|---|---|---|
85	| empty / nonexistent | create dual-repo from scratch | create POR |
86	| POR (jj+git, no config) | upgrade to dual | error (already POR) |
87	| POR (git only, no `.jj/`) | bootstrap `jj git init`, then upgrade | error (already POR-shape) |
88	| code POR + `.claude/` peer POR (configs missing) | write configs only | error |
89	| dual-repo (configs present) | error (already there) | error (downgrade) |
90	| single-repo (illegal shape) | error | error |
91	| anything else (random files, etc.) | error with diagnostic | error with diagnostic |
92	
93	**Clone** is *dumb* — it just clones URLs into target dirs.
94	
95	| `--scope=por` | clone the URL into `<target>` |
96	| `--scope=code,bot` | clone code URL into `<target>`, derive bot URL (`<URL>.claude`), clone into `<target>/.claude/`, then create symlink via `vc-x1 symlink` |
97	
98	For both clone modes: target must NOT exist (`vc-x1 clone`
99	errors if anything is there). Use `vc-x1 sync` to update an
100	existing checkout.
101	
102	`.vc-config.toml` files for the dual-repo case are *tracked
103	content in the remotes*, not files clone writes. They ride
104	along with the clone. If a remote doesn't have them
105	committed, `vc-x1 init <path> --scope=code,bot` from inside
106	the cloned dir handles the config-only upgrade case (see
107	init's state table above).
108	
109	`--scope=code,bot` is implemented as in-process composition
110	of the `--scope=por` primitive (called twice: once for code
111	URL, once for derived bot URL) plus a `symlink::create`
112	call. No subprocess overhead.
113	
114	If init or clone fails partway through, manual cleanup is
115	required (no rollback).
116	
117	### Example Layout local repos
118	
119	cwd = ~/prgs
120	
121	`vc-init test-repos/tf1 --scope=code,bot`
122	`vc-init test-repos/tf1`
123	
124	TODO: add actual output of ls or tree cmds (fill at close-out)
125	
126	`vc-init ../test-repos/tf1 --scope=code,bot`
127	`vc-init ../test-repos/tf1`
128	
129	TODO: add actual output of ls or tree cmds (fill at close-out)
130	
131	### Preflight
132	
133	- Verify `jj` is installed (`jj --version`); friendly error
134	  with install link if missing.
135	- For init URL / `owner/name` forms: probe with `git
136	  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → create via
137	  `gh repo create` (errors if host isn't GitHub or `gh`
138	  missing).
139	- For clone URL / `owner/name` forms: probe with `git
140	  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → error
141	  (clone is dumb, no auto-create).
142	
143	### Edits
144	
145	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.1`.
146	- New `src/repo_url.rs` (or fold into `src/common.rs`): lift
147	  `derive_name` and `resolve_url` from `clone.rs`. Add
148	  `parse_target(s) -> Target` enum `{Url(String),
149	  OwnerName(String, String), Path(PathBuf)}`. Single source
150	  of truth for positional parsing across init and clone.
151	- `src/clone.rs`:
152	  - `CloneArgs`: `<TARGET>` + optional `[NAME]` positionals;
153	    add `--scope code,bot|por` (default `code,bot`); drop
154	    `--dir`. Old `--repo` positional rename: `repo` →
155	    `target`.
156	  - Refactor body into `clone_one(url, target_dir)` (the
157	    primitive, also used by `--scope=por`) and
158	    `clone_dual(code_url, target_dir)` (orchestrator —
159	    derives bot URL, calls `clone_one` twice, calls
160	    `symlink::create`).
161	  - Pre-clone check: error if `target_dir` exists.
162	- `src/init.rs`:
163	  - `InitArgs`: drop `owner`, `repo_local`, `repo_remote`,
164	    `dir`; replace bare-name `name` positional with
165	    `<TARGET>` + optional `[NAME]`.
166	  - Remove the 6 mutually-exclusive flag checks.
167	  - New `detect_state(path) -> InitTargetState`
168	    `{Empty, PorJjGit, PorGitOnly, PorWithPeerPor,
169	    SingleRepoWorkspace, DualRepoWorkspace, Other(reason)}`.
170	  - Refactor body into `init_one(target_dir, opts)` (the
171	    `--scope=por` primitive, also used as a step inside
172	    `--scope=code,bot`) and `init_dual(target_dir, opts)`
173	    (orchestrator — calls `init_one` twice, writes both
174	    `.vc-config.toml` files, ochid-links, `symlink::create`).
175	  - POR upgrade paths:
176	    - PorJjGit + `--scope=code,bot` → skip code-side init,
177	      run bot-side `init_one` into `.claude/`, write configs,
178	      ochid-link existing code commit + new bot commit,
179	      symlink.
180	    - PorGitOnly + `--scope=code,bot` → `jj git init` first,
181	      then PorJjGit path.
182	    - PorWithPeerPor + `--scope=code,bot` → write only the
183	      two `.vc-config.toml` files + symlink (both repos
184	      already exist).
185	- `src/test_helpers.rs`: `Fixture::new_opts` reshapes to use
186	  the new `target` positional shape (and `[NAME]` if needed).
187	- `notes/todo.md`: add this cycle to In Progress.
188	- `notes/chores-07.md`: per-step post-impl subsections.
189	- `notes/vc-x1-init.md`: close out cosmetic anomalies (fold
190	  into final close-out).
191	
192	### Cycle structure — multi-step
193	
194	- `-0` — plan + version bump + this notes section.
195	- `-1` — lift `derive_name` / `resolve_url` / `parse_target`
196	  to shared module; clone migrates internally (no behavior
197	  change yet).
198	- `-2` — clone reshape: `<TARGET>` + `[NAME]` positionals,
199	  add `--scope code,bot|por`, refactor into `clone_one` /
200	  `clone_dual`. Add target-exists pre-check.
201	- `-3` — init reshape: drop old flags, add `<TARGET>` +
202	  `[NAME]`, add `--scope=por`, refactor into `init_one` /
203	  `init_dual`. Existing create-from-empty operations work
204	  via the new shape.
205	- `-4` — init POR detection + upgrade paths
206	  (PorJjGit, PorGitOnly auto-bootstrap, PorWithPeerPor
207	  config-only).
208	- `-5` — `test_helpers::Fixture` migration; audit downstream
209	  callers across the test suite.
210	- final — cycle close-out: fill in Example Layout outputs,
211	  address `notes/vc-x1-init.md` cosmetic anomalies, drop
212	  the `-N` suffix.
213	
214	### Decisions made during design
215	
216	- **Version + cycle line.** This work + the sync `--check`
217	  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
218	  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the
219	  in-flight 0.42.0 work on top of both.
220	- **Path-prefix vocabulary.** `./NAME` and the standard
221	  prefixes (`../`, `/`, `~/`, `~`) only. Bare `.` and bare
222	  `NAME` are errors — explicit prefix required.
223	- **`--private` on existing remote.** Warn and ignore;
224	  visibility was set at create time.
225	- **Cosmetic anomalies** from `notes/vc-x1-init.md` —
226	  addressed at close-out, not deferred.
227	- **`--scope=code` and `--scope=bot` for clone.** Dropped
228	  from the menu. Manual decomposition (two `--scope=por`
229	  clones + `vc-x1 symlink`) covers the use case.
230	- **Composition over duplication.** `--scope=code,bot` is
231	  implemented as in-process composition of the `--scope=por`
232	  primitive — single source of truth for the actual
233	  clone/init operation, thin wrapper for the dual case.
234	
235	## Substep protocol formalization (0.42.0-4.5)
236	
237	Notes-only side cycle. Branched off `main` at 0.42.0-4
238	(init+clone redesign capture); landed between 0.42.0-4
239	and the planned 0.42.0-5 (finalize --scope). Formalizes
240	the substep protocol first exercised in 0.41.1-6.5,
241	validates the close-out squash recipe, and pulls the
242	supporting jj revset vocabulary into a sibling cheatsheet
243	so the protocol can refer to it without duplicating.
244	
245	Version path: numbered `0.42.0-4.5` rather than `0.42.1-0`
246	to avoid pre-empting the patch line while 0.42.0 is
247	mid-cycle. The `.5` suffix positions this as side work
248	between -4 and -5 — semver-legal (pre-release identifiers
249	are dot-separated numeric components and compare
250	numerically) and keeps Cargo.toml's version monotonically
251	increasing.
252	
253	The user opened the cycle by drafting `notes/jj-revsets.md`
254	from their own learning notes (using `notes/substep-test.sh`
255	to build a scratch ladder under `/tmp/substep-test`). The
256	bot reviewed, proposed the substep protocol formalization,
257	and the work landed in five substeps using the protocol it
258	was documenting — dogfood validation in flight.
259	
260	### Edits
261	
262	- `notes/jj-revsets.md`: new. Revset primitives cheatsheet
263	  (chid/cid stability, `@`/`@-`/`@+`, `..`/`::` ranges,
264	  prefix matching). User-authored intro and worked
265	  examples; bot-added `### Interpretation` blocks
266	  cataloguing the operator semantics from the empirical
267	  data, two review passes for typos and grammar.
268	- `notes/substep-protocol.md`: new. Formal protocol of
269	  record. Five sections — purpose, per-substep contract
270	  (`cargo test --bins` non-negotiable), navigation
271	  (cross-link to jj-revsets.md), close-out, recovery —
272	  plus a worked end-to-end example. Drops the prior
273	  "draft text / working hypothesis" hedges. Close-out
274	  recipe validated against scratch ladders at N=3 and N=5
275	  substeps:
276	  ```
277	  jj squash --from "<base>..@-" --into @ -u -R .
278	  ```
279	- `notes/substep-test.sh`: new. Four-revision ladder
280	  scaffold under `/tmp/substep-test`. Used to validate
281	  the squash recipe; remains as a reusable scratch tool
282	  for future substep experiments.
283	- `CLAUDE.md`: `#### Substeps within a multi-step X.Y.Z-N`
284	  subsection added under `### Versioning`. Points at
285	  `notes/substep-protocol.md` (full procedure) and
286	  `notes/jj-revsets.md` (revset primitives the procedure
287	  relies on). Conservative integration scope; broader
288	  CLAUDE.md reorg candidates (consolidating the
289	  `## Committing` / `## Commit Message Style` /
290	  `## ochid Trailers` triplet, surfacing recovery as its
291	  own section, top-of-file TOC) surfaced in the substep-4
292	  commit body for next-cycle discussion.
293	- `notes/todo.md`: `## Done` entry; reference [73] points
294	  here.
295	- `notes/chores-07.md`: this subsection.
296	
297	### Substep ladder (squashed at close-out)
298	
299	- substep 0: `notes/jj-revsets.md` review fixes (typos,
300	  two `### Interpretation` blocks for relative- and
301	  absolute-revset operators).
302	- substep 1: validate close-out squash recipe against
303	  N=3 and N=5 ladders in `/tmp/substep-test`. No file
304	  changes; finding folded into the next substep.
305	- substep 2: `notes/substep-protocol.md` formal rewrite.
306	- substep 3: `CLAUDE.md` pointer subsection.
307	- substep 4: second-pass review fixes after the user's
308	  in-place tweaks added the concrete `lu::` example and
309	  surfaced typos in the absolute-revsets bullet list.
310	- close-out: this commit. Single squashed commit on
311	  `main` via the validated recipe.
312	
313	### Decisions made during design
314	
315	- **`-R .` explicitness.** The protocol shows `-R .` on
316	  every `jj` invocation. For the bot in a dual-repo
317	  workspace this is correct (always be explicit about
318	  which repo); for human users in a single-repo project
319	  the flag is optional. Closing note in the protocol
320	  spells this out.
321	- **Linear vs parallel substep topology.** The ladder is
322	  linear (`jj new` chains commits). User noted at
323	  close-out that a parallel-then-merge topology
324	  (`jj new -r <chid>` to branch from a specific point)
325	  might give cleaner per-concern diffs. Captured as a
326	  future experiment; the close-out recipe would change
327	  too — `<base>..@-` assumes linear ancestry.
328	- **`jj op restore` as recovery vs close-out.** The draft
329	  protocol had floated `jj op restore` as a close-out
330	  alternative. Validation showed it discards substep
331	  work — it's a recovery tool, not close-out. Final
332	  protocol keeps it under Recovery only.
333	- **`-u` on the close-out squash.** `--use-destination-message`
334	  preserves `@`'s description through the squash (which
335	  `vc-x1 push`'s `commit-app` then overwrites with the
336	  close-out title/body anyway). Without `-u`, jj opens
337	  `$EDITOR` to combine source and destination
338	  descriptions — unwanted at close-out, since none of
339	  the substep titles are meant to ship.
340	
341	## init-clone-refactor recovery (0.42.0-4.6)
342	
343	Notes-only side cycle. Sibling to 0.42.0-4.5 — both
344	land between 0.42.0-4 and the planned 0.42.0-5 while
345	the main ladder is paused. Captures the 2026-05-02
346	recovery of the `init-clone-refactor` branch from a
347	destructive local rewrite and reframes
348	`notes/init-clone-refactor-conflict.md` from a
349	forward-looking brief into a post-mortem + reusable
350	playbook.
351	
352	### Background
353	
354	The branch had been broken for several days: 8 commits
355	in conflict state, branched from 0.41.0, never rebased
356	onto post-0.42.0 main. The original brief (drafted
357	2026-05-01) hypothesized "interrupted rebase or
358	substep-style rewrite" as the cause. Investigation
359	2026-05-02 showed a different cause: the bot thinks a
360	destructive `jj squash` collapsed published `0.41.1-1`,
361	`-2`, `-3` into `-4` (stripping `-4`'s description), and
362	conflicts cascaded down through every descendant. The
363	published remote was untouched and held the
364	canonical-good version of every commit.
365	
366	### Recovery
367	
368	```
369	jj bookmark set init-clone-refactor \
370	  -r init-clone-refactor@origin --allow-backwards -R .
371	```
372	
373	All 13 commits from fork to tip are unconflicted on the
374	recovered branch; `cargo test --bins` passes 331/331.
375	Three commits that had been lost locally (`0.41.1-1`,
376	`-2`, `-3`) reappeared, and `-4`'s real description
377	("user config rewrite — multi-account schema") was
378	restored. Local branch state now matches the published
379	remote exactly — no force push needed when the cycle
380	eventually resumes.
381	
382	Detailed playbook captured in
383	`notes/init-clone-refactor-conflict.md` for reuse if
384	the same shape of breakage happens again. Reusable bits
385	include: the "compare local-only chids vs remote chids"
386	diagnostic, the `heads(::A & ::B)` fork-point query, and
387	the back-up-first → verify → reset → sanity-test
388	sequence.
389	
390	### Edits
391	
392	- `notes/init-clone-refactor-conflict.md`: rewrite.
393	  Reframed from forward-looking brief to post-mortem +
394	  reusable playbook. New sections: "Status as of
395	  2026-05-02", "Root cause", "Recovery playbook",
396	  "Diagnostic technique (reusable)". Trimmed: open
397	  question on cleanup version label (moot — no commits
398	  authored, just a bookmark reset).
399	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.42.0-4.6.
400	- `notes/todo.md`: `## Done` entry; reference [74]
401	  points here.
402	- `notes/chores-07.md`: this subsection.
403	
404	### Decisions made during recovery
405	
406	- **Reset bookmark vs resolve in place.** Reset wins
407	  because every local-only chid had a clean
408	  counterpart on the remote — zero unique work to
409	  preserve. Resolving 8 conflicted commits in place
410	  would have been mechanical and error-prone for no
411	  benefit.
412	- **Defer merge direction.** Whether `0.41.1` rebases
413	  onto `0.42.0` (→ `0.43.0` or `0.42.1`) vs some
414	  other topology stays open. The recovery just clears
415	  the technical-feasibility risk; the topology call
416	  is independent and can wait.
417	- **Keep the orphan local rewrites.** 8 broken
418	  local-only chids are now bookmark-less orphans in
419	  `jj log`. `jj abandon` cleanup is cosmetic;
420	  deferred.
421	
422	## --scope enum refactor (0.42.0)
423	
424	Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
425	foundation captured in [2] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
426	--scope enum vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
427	type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
428	flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
429	path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
430	new shape in dependency order.
431	
432	**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
433	once each command's call sites are seen).**
434	
435	- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
436	  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
437	- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` enum. Internal only — no
438	  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
439	  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
440	  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
441	  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
442	  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
443	  follow.
444	- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
445	  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
446	  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
447	  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
448	  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
449	  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
450	  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
451	  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
452	  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
453	- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
454	  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
455	  three-state model from [2]: dual workspace →
456	  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
457	  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
458	  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
459	  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
460	  exercise the resolver chain.
461	- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
462	  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
463	  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
464	  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
465	  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
466	  modes work as captured in chores-06 [1]. Persist scope
467	  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
468	  integration tests for each scope shape.
469	- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
470	  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
471	  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
472	  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
473	  intentional break, not a deprecation.
474	- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
475	  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
476	  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
477	- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
478	  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
479	  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
480	  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
481	  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
482	- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
483	  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
484	  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.
485	
486	**Deferred to later cycles (per [2]).**
487	
488	- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
489	  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
490	  two repos by definition).
491	- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
492	  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
493	  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
494	- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
495	  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.
496	
497	**References.** [2] points back at chores-06's
498	`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope enum vocabulary` for
499	the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
500	applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
501	diving into any of the `-N` steps below.
502	
503	### 0.42.0-1: scope.rs enum
504	
505	Internal-only refactor: tuple-struct `Scope(Vec<Side>)` →
506	`enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`. No CLI
507	surface changes, no consumer logic changes — every existing
508	caller still constructs and consumes `Roles(_)`; `Single(_)`
509	is staged for the parser (-2) and the sync resolver (-3).
510	
511	- `src/scope.rs`: enum body + `PathBuf` import; helpers
512	  (`has_code` / `has_bot` / `is_*_only` / `is_both` /
513	  `is_empty`) reflect the `Roles` arm via `matches!`,
514	  returning `false` (resp. not-empty) on `Single(_)`. New
515	  test pins the `Single(_)` helper behavior; existing
516	  Roles tests retitled accordingly. `#[allow(dead_code)]`
517	  on the `Single` variant for the staging window.
518	- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` returns
519	  `Scope::Roles(_)`; `scope_to_repos` matches on the enum
520	  and returns an explicit error for `Single(_)` carrying
521	  the `0.42.0-3` staging marker. New test locks that
522	  contract so the -3 wire-up is forced to update it.
523	- `src/sync.rs`: `Some(sides) => Scope::Roles(sides.clone())`
524	  in `resolve_args_to_repos`; integration-test
525	  constructors switch to `Scope::Roles(...)`.
526	- `src/init.rs`: `plan_init` constructs `Scope::Roles(...)`
527	  for both the default and explicit-flag paths. Helper
528	  call sites (`is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_bot_only`)
529	  unchanged — the methods continue to work on `Roles`.
530	
531	Build is warning-clean; 257 tests pass (255 baseline + 2
532	new in scope/common).
533	
534	### 0.42.0-2: --scope parser + retrofit init
535	
536	Lands the custom CLI value parser and migrates `init` onto
537	the new `Scope` shape. Parser is the shared boundary every
538	remaining `--scope` consumer (sync, push, finalize, clone)
539	will route through in -3+; init is the smallest first
540	consumer and exercises the keyword path end-to-end. The
541	path form is accepted by the parser but rejected at init
542	with a hint pointing at `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`
543	(init creates a workspace, not a single-repo project).
544	
545	- `src/scope.rs`: `pub fn parse_scope(s: &str) -> Result<Scope, String>`.
546	  Accepts the four keyword forms (`code`, `bot`, `code,bot`,
547	  `bot,code`) plus path forms prefixed by `./`, `../`, `/`,
548	  `~/`, or the bare `~`. Anything else errors with a hint
549	  pointing at the prefix-disambiguation rule. Order is
550	  preserved on the keyword forms. Drops the now-dead
551	  `is_empty` helper (parser rejects empty input upstream;
552	  the only consumer that called it was init's redundant
553	  validation). 13 new parser unit tests.
554	- `src/init.rs`: `--scope` field flips from
555	  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (clap `value_delimiter = ','`) to
556	  `Option<Scope>` with `value_parser = crate::scope::parse_scope`.
557	  `plan_init` matches on the parsed `Scope`: `Roles(_)`
558	  takes the existing bot-only-fatal path; `Single(_)` is
559	  rejected with the init-specific hint. Test scaffolding
560	  (`fixture_scoped`) and 5 fixture call sites migrated.
561	  `scope_parses_*` tests retitled around the new field
562	  type. New `scope_path_form_rejected_at_init` test pins
563	  the rejection contract.
564	
565	Smoke-tested end-to-end via CLI:
566	
567	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code --dry-run` → succeeds
568	  (single-repo dry-run plan).
569	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code,bot --dry-run` → succeeds.
570	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope bot --dry-run` → bot-only fatal
571	  (existing `plan_init` check).
572	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope ./foo --dry-run` → path-form
573	  fatal (new `plan_init` check).
574	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope '~/work' --dry-run` → path-form
575	  fatal.
576	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope foo --dry-run` → clap-level
577	  parser error with hint about `./foo` disambiguation.
578	
579	### 0.42.0-3: sync --scope retrofit
580	
581	Migrates `sync` onto the new `Scope` shape and lands the
582	`Single(_)` resolver wiring. `-R/--repo` retires (single-
583	repo callers move to `--scope=<path>`); `-s` becomes the
584	short form of `--scope`. Default-scope rules pick up the
585	three-state model captured in chores-06's [1] (-4
586	vocabulary): dual workspace → `Roles([Code, Bot])`,
587	single-repo workspace → `Roles([Code])`, POR →
588	`Single(cwd)`.
589	
590	- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` signature gains a `cwd`
591	  parameter; the POR branch (no workspace_root) now
592	  returns `Scope::Single(cwd.to_path_buf())` instead of
593	  `Roles([Code])`. `scope_to_repos`'s `Single(p)` arm
594	  resolves to `vec![p.clone()]` (no workspace lookup;
595	  shell-style expansion happens at the parser/consumer
596	  boundary). The `0.42.0-3` staging-error test is
597	  replaced with `scope_to_repos_single_returns_path` —
598	  the happy-path contract — and `default_scope_por`
599	  becomes `default_scope_por_returns_single_cwd`.
600	- `src/sync.rs`: `--repo`/`-R` flag and the `repos:
601	  Vec<PathBuf>` field deleted. `--scope` retypes from
602	  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (with `value_delimiter = ','`) to
603	  `Option<Scope>` (with `value_parser = parse_scope`)
604	  and gains `short = 's'`. SyncArgs doc rewritten around
605	  the new resolution rules. `resolve_args_to_repos`
606	  drops the `args.repos` branch and now feeds `cwd` into
607	  `default_scope`. `split_repos` and its 4 unit tests
608	  go away with `-R`; `parse_scope_repo_conflict`,
609	  `parse_single_repo_flag`, `parse_repeated_repo_flag`
610	  too. `parse_scope_*` tests assert against `Some(Scope)`;
611	  new `parse_scope_path_form` and `parse_scope_short_form`
612	  tests pin the new entry points. `apply_args` integration
613	  helper drops the `repos` field. `Side` import is gated
614	  `#[cfg(test)]` (production sync no longer needs it
615	  directly — only the tests construct `Side::*`).
616	
617	Smoke-tested end-to-end:
618	
619	- `vc-x1 sync --check` in this dual workspace → `2 repos,
620	  all bookmarks up-to-date`.
621	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s code` → `1 repo, all bookmarks
622	  up-to-date`.
623	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s bot` → ditto for `.claude`.
624	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s ./.claude` → also `1 repo` —
625	  Single-mode resolves directly to that path, bypassing
626	  the workspace's other-repo lookup.
627	- `cd /tmp && vc-x1 sync --check` → POR detected,
628	  attempts to sync `/tmp` (errors because `/tmp` isn't a
629	  jj repo — confirms `default_scope` returned
630	  `Single(cwd)`).
631	
632	# References
633	
634	[1]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
635	[2]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-enum-vocabulary

# Chores-07.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## init + clone redesign (0.41.1)

Empirical validation 2026-04-27 against `vc-x1 0.42.0-3` (see
`notes/vc-x1-init.md`) surfaced cosmetic anomalies and a
substantive design gap. Init's flag surface (`--repo-local`,
`--repo-remote`, `--owner`, `--dir`, `[NAME]`) carries 6+
mutually-exclusive preflight checks just to prevent impossible
combinations from being typed. Clone already implements a
unified-positional alternative (URL / `owner/name` shorthand);
this cycle extends that pattern to init, adds POR support to
both, and unifies their CLI surfaces.

Branched off 0.41.0 (commit `6747a27`). On close-out, the
in-flight 0.42.0 work rebases on top.

### Command structure

```
vc-x1 init  <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por] [--private] [--dry-run]
vc-x1 clone <TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por]             [--dry-run]
```

Identical surfaces modulo `--private` (init only).

`<TARGET>` accepts:

- **URL** — `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
  (detected by `://` or SSH `<host>:<path>` shape with `@`).
- **`owner/name` shorthand** — single `/`, no path prefix;
  resolved to `git@github.com:owner/name.git`.
- **Path-prefixed** — `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`. Path
  IS the target; last component is the workspace name. Bare
  `.` and bare `NAME` are errors (require explicit prefix).

`[NAME]`:

- For URL / `owner/name` forms: overrides the derived
  destination dir name. `vc-x1 clone owner/foo my-name`
  clones into `./my-name/` instead of `./foo/`.
- For path-prefix form: error if combined (path already
  specifies the full target).
- Path-form for clone: error (clone needs a remote URL;
  see "Operations" for clone's target rules).

`--scope`:

- `code,bot` (default): both repos, dual-repo layout.
- `por`: single repo, no `.vc-config.toml` written.
- `code` / `bot` standalone: error. Reason — these are
  config-lookup keywords; init has no config to look up
  against, and clone's simplification omits them too. The
  manual decomposition (clone code as POR, clone bot as
  POR, place at `.claude/`, run `vc-x1 symlink`) covers
  the rare cases where a user wants the dual layout
  composed by hand.

`--private`: only meaningful for init when creating a new
GitHub repo. Sets visibility at `gh repo create`. Silently
warn-and-ignore if the remote already exists. Errors on
clone (clone never creates a remote).

`--dry-run`: validate the operation as far as possible
without side effects. Print what would be done.

### Operations

**Init** is *smart* — it detects the target dir's state and
chooses the right operation.

| Target state | `--scope=code,bot` | `--scope=por` |
|---|---|---|
| empty / nonexistent | create dual-repo from scratch | create POR |
| POR (jj+git, no config) | upgrade to dual | error (already POR) |
| POR (git only, no `.jj/`) | bootstrap `jj git init`, then upgrade | error (already POR-shape) |
| code POR + `.claude/` peer POR (configs missing) | write configs only | error |
| dual-repo (configs present) | error (already there) | error (downgrade) |
| single-repo (illegal shape) | error | error |
| anything else (random files, etc.) | error with diagnostic | error with diagnostic |

**Clone** is *dumb* — it just clones URLs into target dirs.

| `--scope=por` | clone the URL into `<target>` |
| `--scope=code,bot` | clone code URL into `<target>`, derive bot URL (`<URL>.claude`), clone into `<target>/.claude/`, then create symlink via `vc-x1 symlink` |

For both clone modes: target must NOT exist (`vc-x1 clone`
errors if anything is there). Use `vc-x1 sync` to update an
existing checkout.

`.vc-config.toml` files for the dual-repo case are *tracked
content in the remotes*, not files clone writes. They ride
along with the clone. If a remote doesn't have them
committed, `vc-x1 init <path> --scope=code,bot` from inside
the cloned dir handles the config-only upgrade case (see
init's state table above).

`--scope=code,bot` is implemented as in-process composition
of the `--scope=por` primitive (called twice: once for code
URL, once for derived bot URL) plus a `symlink::create`
call. No subprocess overhead.

If init or clone fails partway through, manual cleanup is
required (no rollback).

`vc-init test-repos/tf1`

  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → create via
  `gh repo create` (errors if host isn't GitHub or `gh`
  missing).
- For clone URL / `owner/name` forms: probe with `git
  ls-remote <url>`. Exists → clone. Doesn't → error
  (clone is dumb, no auto-create).

## Substep protocol formalization (0.42.0-4.5)

Notes-only side cycle. Branched off `main` at 0.42.0-4
(init+clone redesign capture); landed between 0.42.0-4
and the planned 0.42.0-5 (finalize --scope). Formalizes
the substep protocol first exercised in 0.41.1-6.5,
validates the close-out squash recipe, and pulls the
supporting jj revset vocabulary into a sibling cheatsheet
so the protocol can refer to it without duplicating.

Version path: numbered `0.42.0-4.5` rather than `0.42.1-0`
to avoid pre-empting the patch line while 0.42.0 is
mid-cycle. The `.5` suffix positions this as side work
between -4 and -5 — semver-legal (pre-release identifiers
are dot-separated numeric components and compare
numerically) and keeps Cargo.toml's version monotonically
increasing.

The user opened the cycle by drafting `notes/jj-revsets.md`
from their own learning notes (using `notes/substep-test.sh`
to build a scratch ladder under `/tmp/substep-test`). The
bot reviewed, proposed the substep protocol formalization,
and the work landed in five substeps using the protocol it
was documenting — dogfood validation in flight.

### Edits

- `notes/jj-revsets.md`: new. Revset primitives cheatsheet
  (chid/cid stability, `@`/`@-`/`@+`, `..`/`::` ranges,
  prefix matching). User-authored intro and worked
  examples; bot-added `### Interpretation` blocks
  cataloguing the operator semantics from the empirical
  data, two review passes for typos and grammar.
- `notes/substep-protocol.md`: new. Formal protocol of
  record. Five sections — purpose, per-substep contract
  (`cargo test --bins` non-negotiable), navigation
  (cross-link to jj-revsets.md), close-out, recovery —
  plus a worked end-to-end example. Drops the prior
  "draft text / working hypothesis" hedges. Close-out
  recipe validated against scratch ladders at N=3 and N=5
  substeps:
  ```
  jj squash --from "<base>..@-" --into @ -u -R .
  ```
- `notes/substep-test.sh`: new. Four-revision ladder
  scaffold under `/tmp/substep-test`. Used to validate
  the squash recipe; remains as a reusable scratch tool
  for future substep experiments.
- `CLAUDE.md`: `#### Substeps within a multi-step X.Y.Z-N`
  subsection added under `### Versioning`. Points at
  `notes/substep-protocol.md` (full procedure) and
  `notes/jj-revsets.md` (revset primitives the procedure
  relies on). Conservative integration scope; broader
  CLAUDE.md reorg candidates (consolidating the
  `## Committing` / `## Commit Message Style` /
  `## ochid Trailers` triplet, surfacing recovery as its
  own section, top-of-file TOC) surfaced in the substep-4
  commit body for next-cycle discussion.
- `notes/todo.md`: `## Done` entry; reference [73] points
  here.
- `notes/chores-07.md`: this subsection.

### Substep ladder (squashed at close-out)

- substep 0: `notes/jj-revsets.md` review fixes (typos,
  two `### Interpretation` blocks for relative- and
  absolute-revset operators).
- substep 1: validate close-out squash recipe against
  N=3 and N=5 ladders in `/tmp/substep-test`. No file
  changes; finding folded into the next substep.
- substep 2: `notes/substep-protocol.md` formal rewrite.
- substep 3: `CLAUDE.md` pointer subsection.
- substep 4: second-pass review fixes after the user's
  in-place tweaks added the concrete `lu::` example and
  surfaced typos in the absolute-revsets bullet list.
- close-out: this commit. Single squashed commit on
  `main` via the validated recipe.

### Decisions made during design

- **`-R .` explicitness.** The protocol shows `-R .` on
  every `jj` invocation. For the bot in a dual-repo
  workspace this is correct (always be explicit about
  which repo); for human users in a single-repo project
  the flag is optional. Closing note in the protocol
  spells this out.
- **Linear vs parallel substep topology.** The ladder is
  linear (`jj new` chains commits). User noted at
  close-out that a parallel-then-merge topology
  (`jj new -r <chid>` to branch from a specific point)
  might give cleaner per-concern diffs. Captured as a
  future experiment; the close-out recipe would change
  too — `<base>..@-` assumes linear ancestry.
- **`jj op restore` as recovery vs close-out.** The draft
  protocol had floated `jj op restore` as a close-out
  alternative. Validation showed it discards substep
  work — it's a recovery tool, not close-out. Final
  protocol keeps it under Recovery only.
- **`-u` on the close-out squash.** `--use-destination-message`
  preserves `@`'s description through the squash (which
  `vc-x1 push`'s `commit-app` then overwrites with the
  close-out title/body anyway). Without `-u`, jj opens
  `$EDITOR` to combine source and destination
  descriptions — unwanted at close-out, since none of
  the substep titles are meant to ship.

## init-clone-refactor recovery (0.42.0-4.6)

Notes-only side cycle. Sibling to 0.42.0-4.5 — both
land between 0.42.0-4 and the planned 0.42.0-5 while
the main ladder is paused. Captures the 2026-05-02
recovery of the `init-clone-refactor` branch from a
destructive local rewrite and reframes
`notes/init-clone-refactor-conflict.md` from a
forward-looking brief into a post-mortem + reusable
playbook.

### Background

The branch had been broken for several days: 8 commits
in conflict state, branched from 0.41.0, never rebased
onto post-0.42.0 main. The original brief (drafted
2026-05-01) hypothesized "interrupted rebase or
substep-style rewrite" as the cause. Investigation
2026-05-02 showed a different cause: the bot thinks a
destructive `jj squash` collapsed published `0.41.1-1`,
`-2`, `-3` into `-4` (stripping `-4`'s description), and
conflicts cascaded down through every descendant. The
published remote was untouched and held the
canonical-good version of every commit.

### Recovery

```
jj bookmark set init-clone-refactor \
  -r init-clone-refactor@origin --allow-backwards -R .
```

All 13 commits from fork to tip are unconflicted on the
recovered branch; `cargo test --bins` passes 331/331.
Three commits that had been lost locally (`0.41.1-1`,
`-2`, `-3`) reappeared, and `-4`'s real description
("user config rewrite — multi-account schema") was
restored. Local branch state now matches the published
remote exactly — no force push needed when the cycle
eventually resumes.

Detailed playbook captured in
`notes/init-clone-refactor-conflict.md` for reuse if
the same shape of breakage happens again. Reusable bits
include: the "compare local-only chids vs remote chids"
diagnostic, the `heads(::A & ::B)` fork-point query, and
the back-up-first → verify → reset → sanity-test
sequence.

### Edits

- `notes/init-clone-refactor-conflict.md`: rewrite.
  Reframed from forward-looking brief to post-mortem +
  reusable playbook. New sections: "Status as of
  2026-05-02", "Root cause", "Recovery playbook",
  "Diagnostic technique (reusable)". Trimmed: open
  question on cleanup version label (moot — no commits
  authored, just a bookmark reset).
- `Cargo.toml`: bump to 0.42.0-4.6.
- `notes/todo.md`: `## Done` entry; reference [74]
  points here.
- `notes/chores-07.md`: this subsection.

### Decisions made during recovery

- **Reset bookmark vs resolve in place.** Reset wins
  because every local-only chid had a clean
  counterpart on the remote — zero unique work to
  preserve. Resolving 8 conflicted commits in place
  would have been mechanical and error-prone for no
  benefit.
- **Defer merge direction.** Whether `0.41.1` rebases
  onto `0.42.0` (→ `0.43.0` or `0.42.1`) vs some
  other topology stays open. The recovery just clears
  the technical-feasibility risk; the topology call
  is independent and can wait.
- **Keep the orphan local rewrites.** 8 broken
  local-only chids are now bookmark-less orphans in
  `jj log`. `jj abandon` cleanup is cosmetic;
  deferred.

--scope enum vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
new shape in dependency order.

once each command's call sites are seen).**

  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.

  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing

  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,

- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add

  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on

### 0.42.0-2: --scope parser + retrofit init

Lands the custom CLI value parser and migrates `init` onto
the new `Scope` shape. Parser is the shared boundary every
remaining `--scope` consumer (sync, push, finalize, clone)
will route through in -3+; init is the smallest first
consumer and exercises the keyword path end-to-end. The
path form is accepted by the parser but rejected at init
with a hint pointing at `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`
(init creates a workspace, not a single-repo project).

- `src/scope.rs`: `pub fn parse_scope(s: &str) -> Result<Scope, String>`.
  Accepts the four keyword forms (`code`, `bot`, `code,bot`,
  `bot,code`) plus path forms prefixed by `./`, `../`, `/`,
  `~/`, or the bare `~`. Anything else errors with a hint
  pointing at the prefix-disambiguation rule. Order is
  preserved on the keyword forms. Drops the now-dead
  `is_empty` helper (parser rejects empty input upstream;
  the only consumer that called it was init's redundant
  validation). 13 new parser unit tests.
- `src/init.rs`: `--scope` field flips from
  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (clap `value_delimiter = ','`) to
  `Option<Scope>` with `value_parser = crate::scope::parse_scope`.
  `plan_init` matches on the parsed `Scope`: `Roles(_)`
  takes the existing bot-only-fatal path; `Single(_)` is
  rejected with the init-specific hint. Test scaffolding
  (`fixture_scoped`) and 5 fixture call sites migrated.
  `scope_parses_*` tests retitled around the new field
  type. New `scope_path_form_rejected_at_init` test pins
  the rejection contract.

Smoke-tested end-to-end via CLI:

- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code --dry-run` → succeeds
  (single-repo dry-run plan).
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code,bot --dry-run` → succeeds.
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope bot --dry-run` → bot-only fatal
  (existing `plan_init` check).
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope ./foo --dry-run` → path-form
  fatal (new `plan_init` check).
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope '~/work' --dry-run` → path-form
  fatal.
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope foo --dry-run` → clap-level
  parser error with hint about `./foo` disambiguation.

three-state model captured in chores-06's [1] (-4

single-repo workspace → `Roles([Code])`, POR →

  returns `Scope::Single(cwd.to_path_buf())` instead of
  `Roles([Code])`. `scope_to_repos`'s `Single(p)` arm
  resolves to `vec![p.clone()]` (no workspace lookup;
  shell-style expansion happens at the parser/consumer
  boundary). The `0.42.0-3` staging-error test is
  replaced with `scope_to_repos_single_returns_path` —
  the happy-path contract — and `default_scope_por`
  becomes `default_scope_por_returns_single_cwd`.
- `src/sync.rs`: `--repo`/`-R` flag and the `repos:
  Vec<PathBuf>` field deleted. `--scope` retypes from
  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (with `value_delimiter = ','`) to
  `Option<Scope>` (with `value_parser = parse_scope`)
  and gains `short = 's'`. SyncArgs doc rewritten around
  the new resolution rules. `resolve_args_to_repos`
  drops the `args.repos` branch and now feeds `cwd` into
  `default_scope`. `split_repos` and its 4 unit tests
  go away with `-R`; `parse_scope_repo_conflict`,
  `parse_single_repo_flag`, `parse_repeated_repo_flag`
  too. `parse_scope_*` tests assert against `Some(Scope)`;
  new `parse_scope_path_form` and `parse_scope_short_form`
  tests pin the new entry points. `apply_args` integration
  helper drops the `repos` field. `Side` import is gated
  `#[cfg(test)]` (production sync no longer needs it
  directly — only the tests construct `Side::*`).

Smoke-tested end-to-end:

- `vc-x1 sync --check` in this dual workspace → `2 repos,
  all bookmarks up-to-date`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s code` → `1 repo, all bookmarks
  up-to-date`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s bot` → ditto for `.claude`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s ./.claude` → also `1 repo` —
  Single-mode resolves directly to that path, bypassing
  the workspace's other-repo lookup.
- `cd /tmp && vc-x1 sync --check` → POR detected,
  attempts to sync `/tmp` (errors because `/tmp` isn't a
  jj repo — confirms `default_scope` returned
  `Single(cwd)`).

# References

[1]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
[2]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-enum-vocabulary

# init-clone-refactor: recovery post-mortem + playbook

Drafted 2026-05-01 as a forward-looking brief while the
branch was still broken; rewritten 2026-05-02 after the
recovery completed. Documents what went wrong, the
playbook that fixed it, and the strategic decisions
still open.

## Status as of 2026-05-02 (post-recovery)

- `init-clone-refactor` bookmark is at `a8c1eefe`
  (chid `sumruqqomnzs`) — the published clean
  `0.41.1-6.5` commit.
- All 13 commits from fork point `ykxrlkkwqpyv`
  (`0.41.0`, cid `6747a27b`) through `0.41.1-6.5` are
  unconflicted. `cargo test --bins` passes 331/331 on
  the recovered tip.
- Recovery via `jj bookmark set init-clone-refactor
  -r init-clone-refactor@origin --allow-backwards
  -R .`. The published remote branch turned out to
  be the canonical-good version; local was holding a
  destructively-rewritten copy.
- 8 broken local-only chids still visible as orphans
  in `jj log` (no bookmark, GC-eligible). `jj abandon`
  cleanup is cosmetic, deferrable.

## Status as of 2026-05-01 (pre-recovery, historical)

- Branch existed locally with 8 commits all in
  conflict state: `0.41.1-0` (chore: open) → unnamed
  → `0.41.1-5` (init reshape) → `0.41.1-6.0..6.5`
  (POR baseline tests then progressive split of
  `create_local_repo`). Conflicts on the same files
  all the way down: `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`,
  `notes/todo.md`, `notes/chores-08.md` (3-sided),
  `src/config.rs`, `src/init.rs`, `src/clone.rs`.
- Original brief's hypothesis — "interrupted rebase
  or substep-style rewrite" — turned out to be wrong.
  See "Root cause" below.
- `@origin (ahead by at least 10 commits, behind by
  8 commits)` was what jj reported. The "ahead by
  *at least* 10" caveat (vs the precise "behind by
  8") was the tell that the remote held more commits
  than local — i.e. local had *lost* work, not just
  rewritten it. Worth noticing earlier next time.

## Why this matters (intent)

Refactor `init` and `clone` to share `init_one` /
`clone_one` primitives plus orchestrators
(`init_dual` / `clone_dual`). Eliminates duplicated
lifecycle logic between init and clone, and gives a
single chokepoint for `--scope`-related changes —
important because every subcommand picking up
`--scope` lands on the same `Scope` shape, and
reusable foundations mean those changes happen in
one place rather than parallel implementations.
Design captured in `notes/chores-07.md > ## init +
clone redesign (0.41.1)`.

## Root cause (post-mortem)

The bot thinks the local rewrites came from a
destructive `jj squash` (or equivalent operation)
that collapsed published commits `0.41.1-1`, `-2`,
`-3` into `-4`. Symptoms supporting this:

- Three chids existed on the remote (`mkwqykksykoo`
  `-1`, `plxsuynrtwks` `-2`, `xtrworrlkozn` `-3`)
  with no local counterpart at all.
- `uuzwzxzkszwq` (the local "unnamed" commit) shared
  the *same chid* as the remote `0.41.1-4` commit,
  but had its description stripped — consistent with
  a squash that pulled three commits' content into
  `-4`'s tree but lost the descriptions.
- Conflicts cascaded down `-5` and `-6.0..6.5`
  because those commits' parent tree no longer
  matched what they had been authored against — the
  rewritten `-4` diverged from what the descendants
  expected.

Net effect: 3 commits of real work disappeared from
local, `-4`'s description was lost, and every
descendant inherited a conflict against the changed
parent tree. The remote was untouched and held the
canonical-good version of all of it.

## Strategic decision (still deferred)

Keep `main` + the `0.42.0-X` series as-is. Now that
`init-clone-refactor` is recovered, we have a clean
branch from which to make the merge-direction call:

- rebase `0.41.1` on top of `0.42.0` as a `0.43.0`
  (or `0.42.1`), or
- vice versa (force-rewrite main — only if there's
  a reason that justifies rewriting published
  history),
- or some other topology.

Decision still deferred. The recovery removes the
"is the branch even salvageable" risk; the topology
question is now purely about scope and review
ergonomics, not technical feasibility.

## Recovery playbook (used 2026-05-02)

Reusable for similar "local rewrites broke a branch
that the remote holds in clean form" situations.
All read-only steps come first; mutation only after
verification.

1. **Back up first.** Cheap insurance.
   - `cp -a . ../<dir>-<date>-N` — full snapshot,
     includes `.jj/op` history. Or `rsync -a
     --exclude=target/ ./ ../<dir>/` if `target/`
     bloat matters.
   - `git clone <remote> ../<dir>-clone-<date>-N`
     — canonical published state, jj-free, browsable
     in gitk without local-rewrite confusion.
   - `jj op log -R . > ../<dir>-op-log-<date>-N.txt`
     — text-readable op log, independent of jj.

2. **Verify remote tip is unconflicted (read-only):**
   ```
   jj git fetch -R .
   jj log -R . -r '<fork-chid>::<branch>@origin' \
     -T '... if(conflict, "CONFLICT", "ok") ...'
   ```
   Every line must read `[ok]`. If any are
   `[CONFLICT]`, the remote isn't a safe target and
   this playbook doesn't apply.

3. **Verify local-only commits are all chid-rewrites
   of remote commits (read-only):**
   ```
   jj log -R . -r '<branch>@origin..<branch>' \
     -T 'change_id.short() ++ ...'
   ```
   Every chid here must also appear on the remote.
   If any chid is unique to local, the reset would
   lose work — investigate before proceeding.

4. **Reset bookmark to remote:**
   ```
   jj bookmark set <branch> -r <branch>@origin \
     --allow-backwards -R .
   ```
   The `--allow-backwards` is required because jj
   guards against accidental backwards moves.

5. **Sanity test:**
   ```
   jj new <branch> -R .
   cargo test --bins      # or full cargo clean ; cargo test for paranoia
   ```

6. **Optional cleanup:** `jj abandon` the orphan
   local-only chids if you don't want them in
   `jj log`. Cosmetic; jj's GC eventually prunes
   them anyway.

## Diagnostic technique (reusable)

Two questions to ask when a branch is mysteriously
conflicted:

- **Are the same chids on the remote in clean form?**
  `jj log -r '<fork>::<branch>@origin'` with the
  conflict-status template tells you whether the
  remote has a working version of every commit.
- **Is local missing any chids the remote has?**
  Compare chid lists between
  `<fork>::<branch>@origin` and
  `<fork>::<branch>` — gaps indicate commits the
  local branch *lost* (squash, abandon, or similar).

If both yield "yes, remote clean + local missing
chids," the reset playbook above is the shortest
path. If only the first yields yes (no missing
chids, local just rewrote into broken state), you
can shrink step 1 of the playbook (work-loss risk
is gone) and just reset.

The fork point itself can be derived without
hand-scanning parent columns:
```
jj log -r 'heads(::<branch-A> & ::<branch-B>)'
```
Returns the latest commit that's an ancestor of
both — the divergence point.

## JJ-CONFLICT-README (the thing that shows in gitk)

When jj has a conflicted commit, it serializes the
conflict state into a git-side tree containing:

- `JJ-CONFLICT-README` — text file explaining the
  format.
- `.jjconflict-base-N/` — the base side(s) of the
  three-way merge.
- `.jjconflict-side-N/` — each conflict input.

Plain git tools (gitk, GitHub UI, etc.) display
this as real files. **Recoverable** — `jj` itself
knows the commit is conflicted and `jj resolve`
walks the inputs. Don't `git checkout` a conflicted
commit and start editing files directly; jj's
invariants get confused. The README itself includes
the recovery hint (`jj abandon` if you accidentally
end up in this state).

## Open questions

Merge direction and chores-NN ordering moved to
`notes/todo.md > ## Todo` (top entries) for
discoverability. Remaining technical question:

- **Conflict surface against 0.42.0.** Now that
  `init-clone-refactor` is clean, what's the actual
  diff vs current `main`? `0.42.0-1/2/3` rewrote
  `init.rs` / `scope.rs` / `common.rs`; `0.41.1-5/
  -6.x` also touched those. Real conflicts will
  surface only at the rebase step.

## References

- `notes/chores-07.md > ## init + clone redesign
  (0.41.1)` — full design captured in 0.42.0-4
  (docs-only commit on main).
- `notes/jj-revsets.md` — revset primitives used in
  the diagnostic and playbook above (`..` exclusive
  range, `::` inclusive DAG range, `heads()`,
  template `if(conflict, ...)`).
- `notes/substep-protocol.md` — close-out squash
  recipe for substep ladders. Note: this branch's
  `-6.0..6.5` ladder was authored before the
  protocol was formalized; the protocol postdates
  it.

content.text:
I've read the recovery notes and walked the branch. Here's the picture.

**Branch state — `init-clone-refactor` is recovered and clean:**
- 12 unique commits on top of `0.41.0` (`6747a27b`): `0.41.1-0` … `0.41.1-6.5`
- Tip: `sumruqqo/a8c1eefe` "refactor: split create_local_repo lifecycle (0.41.1-6.5)"
- All commits unconflicted on the recovered branch (matches `init-clone-refactor@origin`)
- Notes claim 331/331 `cargo test --bins` on the tip
- 8 broken local-only chids still hang as orphans (`sumruqqo/0`, `orxupkxs/0`, `zstmxvrs/0` etc., all `(divergent) (conflict)`) — cosmetic, deferred

**What the branch is — design from `chores-07.md > ## init + clone redesign (0.41.1)`:**
- Refactor `init` and `clone` onto shared primitives: `init_one`/`clone_one` + orchestrators `init_dual`/`clone_dual`
- Unify their CLI: `<TARGET> [NAME] [--scope code,bot|por]`, parse-target enum (`Url | OwnerName | Path`)
- Init becomes "smart" (state-detects target); clone stays "dumb"
- Single chokepoint for `--scope` changes — important because the `0.42.0` cycle is rolling `--scope` through every command

**Ladder shipped on the branch (12 commits):**
- `-0` plan + bump · `-1` shared `repo_url` module · `-2` clone reshape + `symlink::install` · `-3` user config module · `-4` user config rewrite (multi-account) · `-5` init reshape + args module · `-6.0` POR baseline tests · `-6.1`…`-6.5` progressive split of `create_local_repo` lifecycle (substep ladder, never closed out)

**Open strategic question (per `init-clone-refactor-conflict.md > ## Strategic decision`):**
Branch was forked from `0.41.0` and never rebased. Main has since shipped `0.42.0-1/2/3` which rewrote `init.rs` / `scope.rs` / `common.rs` — the same files `-5` / `-6.x` touched. Real conflicts only surface at rebase. Three options on the table:
1. Rebase `0.41.1` onto `0.42.0` → ship as `0.43.0` or `0.42.1`
2. Force-rewrite main (only with strong justification — published history)
3. Other topology

Also outstanding: the `-6.0…6.5` substep ladder was authored before the substep protocol was formalized and was never squashed via the close-out recipe. And `chores-08.md` (per-step subsections for these commits) lives on the branch and would need to be reconciled with `chores-06/07` on main.

What angle do you want to dig into — the rebase conflict surface, the substep close-out, or the merge topology call?

content.text:
Good news — nothing new went wrong today. The divergence you're seeing is the **pre-existing** orphan set from the 2026-05-02 recovery, exactly what the recovery notes flagged as "8 broken local-only chids still visible as orphans in `jj log` (no bookmark, GC-eligible). `jj abandon` cleanup is cosmetic, deferrable" (`notes/init-clone-refactor-conflict.md:24-25`).

**Evidence from the op log** (`jj op log`, most recent first):

```
b98e121d  jj undo                              ← undid (5e75d40d)
953b3a04  jj undo                              ← undid (e6eb0629)
5e75d40d  jj edit -r main --ignore-immutable
e6eb0629  jj edit -r init-clone-refactor       ← landed @ on a8c1eefe
b4acd1f7  jj bookmark delete wip
a2a313d7  jj edit -r wip
```

There is no `snapshot working copy` operation between `e6eb0629` and `5e75d40d`, so `a8c1eefe` was never rewritten — `jj edit` only moves `@` onto a commit; rewrites only happen if the working copy contents differ AND a subsequent command takes a snapshot. Neither happened. The two `jj undo`s rolled the operation pointer back to `b4acd1f7`. `@` is now back at `ptxlnuqs a39c903b` on top of main, working copy clean.

**The divergent chids you see** (7 of them, all `0.41.1-5` through `-6.5`):

```
sumruqqo  /a8c1eefe (clean, on init-clone-refactor)  vs  /ae61955c (orphan, conflict)
orxupkxs  /1f743351 (clean)                          vs  /b3341426 (orphan, conflict)
zstmxvrs  /68450fb9 (clean)                          vs  /5e296be3 (orphan, conflict)
znurqzvp  /90ba6ac7 (clean)                          vs  /a2c302ae (orphan, conflict)
puynnkmp  /64f59ae7 (clean)                          vs  /352fd64b (orphan, conflict)
smvxvzyp  /e847772e (clean)                          vs  /8e42f80c (orphan, conflict)
otkprmrn  /f5ec4d8b (clean)                          vs  /8e47deb2 (orphan, conflict)
```

The `/4` (clean) side of each pair came back during the bookmark reset to `init-clone-refactor@origin`. The `/0` (broken) side is the residue from the original destructive `jj squash`. They've been sitting there since 2026-05-02; today's actions didn't add any.

**Confirmation**: `init-clone-refactor` bookmark = `init-clone-refactor@origin` = `a8c1eefe` exactly. No new commits, no force-push pending, no work lost.

If the orphans bug you visually, `jj abandon <cid>` on each of the 7 broken commit IDs (`ae61955c b3341426 5e296be3 a2c302ae 352fd64b 8e42f80c 8e47deb2`) clears them. Cosmetic — they'd GC eventually.

content.text:
Definitive answer via op-log time travel: **the divergence was present at the moment of the recovery.**

Time travel evidence (using `--at-op`):

```
=== one op BEFORE recovery (3a94c671, 2026-05-01 18:23):
sumruqqo/ae61955c   refactor: split create_local_repo lifecycle (0.41.1-6.5)
                    ← only the broken commit visible, no divergence

=== AT the recovery op (9e84fcaf, 2026-05-02 20:30, bookmark set):
sumruqqo/ae61955c   refactor: split create_local_repo lifecycle (0.41.1-6.5)
sumruqqo/a8c1eefe   refactor: split create_local_repo lifecycle (0.41.1-6.5)
                    ← both visible immediately after the bookmark moved
```

And `jj log -r su` errors with the divergent-chid message **at the recovery op** with the identical wording you'd see now:

```
=== jj log -r su --at-op 9e84fcaf9263 ===
Error: Change ID `su` is divergent
Hint: Use change offset to select single revision: su/0, su/4
```

So `jj log -r su` would have errored yesterday too, the moment after `jj bookmark set init-clone-refactor -r init-clone-refactor@origin --allow-backwards`. Today's `jj edit -r init-clone-refactor` + two `jj undo`s left the divergent set unchanged.

**Why this happens**: `jj bookmark set --allow-backwards` only *moves the bookmark*. It does not abandon the commits the bookmark used to point at. The broken ladder (`-6.0` through `-6.5`) is still a connected subgraph in jj's view because each broken commit is the parent of the next, and the head (`ae61955c`) has no living descendants — so it stays visible as an "orphan head". The recovery playbook's optional step 6 ("`jj abandon` if you don't want them in `jj log`") exists exactly because the bookmark reset doesn't clean up the old chain.

**The bot thinks** what you're remembering as "not divergent yesterday" is most likely one of:
- you ran `jj log -r init-clone-refactor` (doesn't error — just resolves the bookmark to `a8c1eefe` — though it does mark the result `(divergent)`), and didn't try the prefix form `-r su`, OR
- you saw the recovery-success message and the working `jj log` graph view, where the orphan ladder appears as a side branch you mentally treated as "old broken stuff" rather than a chid collision.

Either way, no work has been lost, no new orphan was created today, and `init-clone-refactor` still equals `init-clone-refactor@origin` exactly. If the orphans bother you, the playbook's deferred step 6 is one command per cid — or `jj abandon -r 'change_id(sumruqqo)/0 | change_id(orxupkxs)/0 | …'` to take them all in one shot.

- `sumruqqo` is no longer divergent — only `a8c1eefe` remains, with no `(divergent)` marker.

5	This project uses **two separate jj-git repos**:
6	
7	1. **App repo** (`/` — project root): Contains the application source code.
8	2. **Bot session repo** (`/.claude/`): Contains Claude Code session data.
9	
10	Both repos are managed with `jj` (Jujutsu), which coexists with git.
11	
12	## Repo Paths (relative from project root)
13	
14	- App repo: `.` (project root)
15	- Bot session repo: `.claude`
16	  (symlink from `~/.claude/projects/<path-to-project-root>/.claude`)
17	
18	## Working Directory
19	
20	Prefer staying in the project root. Use `-R` flags or absolute paths
21	to target other directories rather than `cd`. If `cd` seems necessary,
22	discuss with the user first — losing track of cwd causes subtle
23	command failures downstream.
24	
25	When invoking shell commands, prefer the shortest unambiguous path —
26	usually relative to cwd (`ls notes/`, not
27	`ls /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/`). Long absolute paths
28	clutter the transcript without adding information. Out-of-workspace
29	paths (`/tmp/...`, `~/.config/...`) stay absolute. Read/Edit/Write
30	tool args also stay absolute — that's a tool-boundary constraint,
31	not a stylistic choice.
32	
33	## Memory
34	
35	Do not use the bot's per-project memory directory
36	(`~/.claude/projects/<path>/memory/`). In a dual-repo setup with
37	CLAUDE.md it provides no capability CLAUDE.md doesn't already cover,
38	and it loses on discoverability:
39	
40	- **CLAUDE.md** — at the repo root, a well-known location for bot
41	  instructions, committed, reviewable, visible to every collaborator
42	  (human or bot).
43	- **Memory directory** — hidden under the user's home, tied to one
44	  machine, invisible to anyone but the bot, never diffed or reviewed.
45	
46	Easy for everyone to find beats convenient for the bot alone. Put
47	durable context in CLAUDE.md (or committed `notes/`) instead.
48	
49	## Speculation marker
50	
51	Durable text the bot writes — CLAUDE.md, `notes/`, commit bodies,
52	chores sections — should stick to observations and direct descriptions
53	of the code or data. If a mechanism, hypothesis, or causal claim
54	enters the text, prefix it with "The bot thinks ..." so a reader can
55	tell the measured from the inferred.
56	
57	**Why:** unmarked speculation reads like evidence, and a future reader
58	(or the bot on a later session) can pick it up as a known fact when
59	it's not. Measured / inferred is a distinction worth keeping visible
60	in the written record.
61	
62	**How to apply:** observations and factual descriptions need no
63	marker. Prefix with "The bot thinks ..." (or a close variant like
64	"The bot's guess is ...") when the claim is a mechanism ("X wins
65	because Y caches better"), a cause ("the drift was due to thermal
66	state"), a prediction ("this should scale linearly"), or any
67	reasoning not directly supported by the data on hand.
68	
69	## Committing
70	
71	Use `-R` (`--repository`) at the end to target the correct repo. Use
72	relative paths to reduce noise. Putting `-R` last keeps the verb/action
73	visible at the start of the command.
74	
75	### App repo
76	```
77	jj commit -m \
78	"title" \
79	-m "body
80	
81	ochid: /.claude/<changeID>" \
82	-R .
83	```
84	
85	### Bot session repo
86	```
87	jj commit -m \
88	"title" \
89	-m "body
90	
91	ochid: /<changeID>" \
92	-R .claude
93	```
94	
95	## jj Basics
96	
97	- `jj st -R .` / `jj st -R .claude` — show working copy status
98	- `jj log -R .` / `jj log -R .claude` — show commit log
99	- `jj commit -m "title" -m "body" -R <repo>` — finalize working copy into a commit
100	- `jj describe -m "title" -m "body" -R <repo>` — set description without committing
101	- `jj git push --bookmark <name> -R <repo>` — push a bookmark (no
102	  `--allow-new` flag; jj pushes new bookmarks without special flags)
103	- In jj, the working copy (@) is always a mutable commit being edited.
104	  `jj commit` finalizes it and creates a new empty working copy on top.
105	- The `.claude` repo always has uncommitted changes during an active
106	  session because session data updates continuously.
107	
108	## Commit Message Style
109	
110	Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) with
111	a version suffix:
112	
113	```
114	<type>: <short description> (<version>)
115	```
116	
117	- **Title**: target ~50 chars, short summary of *what* changed.
118	  Include the version. Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`,
119	  `test`, `docs`, `chore`.
120	- **App-repo body**: short intro paragraph (1–3 sentences), then a
121	  terse bullet list. Each bullet corresponds one-to-one with the
122	  edits structure already documented in `notes/chores-*.md` for
123	  this step — just the file and a one-line gist (e.g.
124	  `README.md: new Overview intro`). Do *not* restate the detail
125	  that lives in chores; the commit body is a scan-able index, not
126	  a duplicate. The chores section is the source of truth.
127	- **Session-repo body**: terse intro + a few session-activity
128	  bullets. Doesn't need to mirror chores since it describes
129	  in-session work, not code changes.
130	- Examples:
131	  - `feat: add fix-ochid subcommand (0.22.0)`
132	  - `fix: fix-ochid prefix bug (0.22.1)`
133	  - `refactor: deduplicate common CLI flags (0.21.1)`
134	
135	## Pre-commit Requirements
136	
137	### User approval
138	
139	Never execute commit, squash, push, or finalize commands without the
140	user's explicit approval. Present changes for review first; only run
141	them after the user confirms. This applies to late changes too —
142	pause for review before squashing into an existing commit.
143	
144	### Review before proposing the commit block
145	
146	After finishing a unit of work, **summarize what changed and stop
147	there**. Do not pre-emptively lay out the Checkpoint-1 commit
148	commands. Wait for the user to signal review is complete before
149	proposing the commit block. Changes during review are the norm,
150	not the exception; proposing commit text too early creates noise
151	and signals that I consider the work done when it usually isn't.
152	
153	This applies per-step in a multi-step flow too — each step gets a
154	review pause before its commit block appears.
155	
156	Signals that review is complete include explicit approval ("let's
157	commit", "looks good, commit it") **and any directive to start the
158	next step** ("do step 4", "next", "go N+1"). In that case the
159	previous step must be committed first — always commit the current
160	step before starting the next; don't ask.
161	
162	### Per-file review checkpoints
163	
164	The "review before commit block" rule applies to in-progress work
165	too, at finer grain. After each file edit, STOP, summarize what
166	changed, and wait for explicit go-ahead before touching the next
167	file or running follow-on commands (cargo test/install, dogfood
168	invocations, `vc-x1 …`, etc.).
169	
170	**Exceptions** — both ride along with the change they accompany,
171	no separate review pause:
172	
173	- **Refactors where code is moving across files** — e.g.
174	  lifting helpers into a new module and updating the original
175	  file's imports in the same change. Reviewing each half in
176	  isolation isn't useful.
177	- **`Cargo.toml` version bumps** that go with a specific code
178	  change.
179	
180	**Why:** the user reviews diffs as work lands. Chaining multiple
181	file edits without pausing forces them to untangle cumulative
182	state instead of inspecting each step in isolation.
183	
184	**How to apply:**
185	
186	- "continue", "go", "do it", "lg" approves *the next unit
187	  only*, not all remaining units of a multi-file plan. A green
188	  light to proceed with a *direction* is not a green light to
189	  skip per-step checkpoints.
190	- After landing a unit: brief summary (2–5 lines) and stop. No
191	  cargo build / test / install / dogfood runs unless the user
192	  explicitly asks.
193	- In addition to (not replacement for) the
194	  Review-before-commit-block rule above.
195	
196	### Notes references
197	
198	Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.

... [363 lines truncated] ...
