content.content:
242	### Cycle structure — multi-step
243	
244	- `-0` — plan + version bump + `notes/forks-multi-user.md`
245	  capture + `notes/chores-08.md` (this section) +
246	  `notes/vc-x1-init.md` brought forward + partner
247	  bookmarks set up.
248	- `-1` — lift `derive_name` / `resolve_url` /
249	  `derive_session_url` to a new `src/repo_url.rs`; stage
250	  `Target` + `parse_target` for `-2`/`-3` consumers; both
251	  `clone` and `init` migrate at their existing call sites
252	  (init.rs had verbatim duplicates worth de-duping in the
253	  same step). No behavior change.
254	- `-2` — clone reshape: `<TARGET>` + `[NAME]`
255	  positionals, add `--scope code,bot|por`, refactor into
256	  `clone_one` / `clone_dual`. Add target-exists pre-check.
257	- `-3` — user config (first cut):
258	  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` reader; flat
259	  `[default]` + `[github]` schema; standalone module.
260	  Superseded by `-4`'s redesign before any consumer
261	  wired in.
262	- `-4` — user config redesign: replace `-3`'s flat
263	  schema with `[account.<name>].repo.category.<cat>`
264	  multi-account / category structure, literal values,
265	  three-level resolution chain. Standalone; `-5` is the
266	  first consumer. See
267	  [`User config`](#user-config-0411-3-redesigned-in-0411-4).
268	- `-5` — init reshape: drop old flags
269	  (`--owner` / `--dir` / `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`),
270	  add `<TARGET>` + `[NAME]`, `--account`, `--repo`,
271	  `--scope code,bot|por`. Refactor into `init_one` /
272	  `init_dual`. Existing create-from-empty operations
273	  work via the new shape.
274	- `-6` — init POR detection + upgrade paths
275	  (PorJjGit, PorGitOnly auto-bootstrap, PorWithPeerPor
276	  config-only).
277	- `-7` — `test_helpers::Fixture` migration; audit
278	  downstream callers across the test suite.
279	- final — cycle close-out: fill in Example Layout outputs,
280	  address `notes/vc-x1-init.md` cosmetic anomalies, document
281	  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` in `README.md` (simplest /
282	  full-one-account / full-two-account examples), drop
283	  the `-N` suffix.
284	
285	### User config (0.41.1-3, redesigned in 0.41.1-4)
286	
287	User-global config at `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
288	(XDG-compliant; honors `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`). Backs init's
289	account- and repo-target resolution. No magic fallbacks:
290	missing file or missing key → predictable error pointing
291	at the exact key to set.
292	
293	`-3` shipped a flat first-cut schema (`[default]
294	repo-remote-provider`, `[github] owner`); `-4` replaces it
295	with the account/category structure below before init
296	becomes the first consumer in `-5`. Since `-3` had no
297	consumers, the rewrite is non-breaking.
298	
299	Schema:
300	
301	```toml
302	[default]
303	account = "home"      # default --account when absent
304	debug   = "trace"     # default --debug value when used without arg
305	
306	[account.home]
307	repo.default          = "remote"                       # default --repo cat when absent
308	repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # value for --repo remote (no =val)
309	repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"              # value for --repo local (no =val)
310	
311	[account.work]
312	repo.default          = "remote"
313	repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
314	repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
315	```
316	
317	Three layers — each level has its own default-finding key:
318	
319	| CLI                       | Step 1: account                | Step 2: category                  | Step 3: value                                    |
320	|---|---|---|---|
321	| (no `--repo`/`--account`) | `[default].account`            | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
322	| `--account <a>`           | `<a>` (CLI)                    | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
323	| `--repo <cat>`            | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
324	| `--repo <cat>=<val>`      | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `<val>` (CLI, literal)                           |
325	
326	Values are **literal targets**, not section-name
327	pointers. For `category = "remote"`, the value is a URL
328	prefix (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category =
329	"local"`, it's the parent dir for fixture bares. Built-in
330	categories `remote` and `local` are recognized; any other
331	category name errors in `-5` (forward-compat: future
332	cycles can add a `kind` field for user-defined categories).
333	
334	Resolution errors (each step has its own message):
335	
336	- Step 1 missing → `no account specified; set [default].account, use --account <name>, or write a top-level [repo] section`.
337	- Step 2 missing → `no default category for account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo].default or use --repo <cat>`.
338	- Step 3 missing → `no value for --repo <cat> in account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo.category].<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<val>`.
339	
340	#### Single-account shorthand: top-level `[repo]`

### Cycle structure — multi-step

- `-0` — plan + version bump + `notes/forks-multi-user.md`
  capture + `notes/chores-08.md` (this section) +
  `notes/vc-x1-init.md` brought forward + partner
  bookmarks set up.
- `-1` — lift `derive_name` / `resolve_url` /
  `derive_session_url` to a new `src/repo_url.rs`; stage
  `Target` + `parse_target` for `-2`/`-3` consumers; both
  `clone` and `init` migrate at their existing call sites
  (init.rs had verbatim duplicates worth de-duping in the
  same step). No behavior change.
- `-2` — clone reshape: `<TARGET>` + `[NAME]`
  positionals, add `--scope code,bot|por`, refactor into
  `clone_one` / `clone_dual`. Add target-exists pre-check.
- `-3` — user config (first cut):
  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` reader; flat
  `[default]` + `[github]` schema; standalone module.
  Superseded by `-4`'s redesign before any consumer
  wired in.
- `-4` — user config redesign: replace `-3`'s flat
  schema with `[account.<name>].repo.category.<cat>`
  multi-account / category structure, literal values,
  three-level resolution chain. Standalone; `-5` is the
  first consumer. See
  [`User config`](#user-config-0411-3-redesigned-in-0411-4).
- `-5` — init reshape: drop old flags
  (`--owner` / `--dir` / `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`),
  add `<TARGET>` + `[NAME]`, `--account`, `--repo`,
  `--scope code,bot|por`. Refactor into `init_one` /
  `init_dual`. Existing create-from-empty operations
  work via the new shape.
- `-6` — init POR detection + upgrade paths
  (PorJjGit, PorGitOnly auto-bootstrap, PorWithPeerPor
  config-only).
- `-7` — `test_helpers::Fixture` migration; audit
  downstream callers across the test suite.
- final — cycle close-out: fill in Example Layout outputs,
  address `notes/vc-x1-init.md` cosmetic anomalies, document
  `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml` in `README.md` (simplest /
  full-one-account / full-two-account examples), drop
  the `-N` suffix.

### User config (0.41.1-3, redesigned in 0.41.1-4)

User-global config at `~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`
(XDG-compliant; honors `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`). Backs init's
account- and repo-target resolution. No magic fallbacks:
missing file or missing key → predictable error pointing
at the exact key to set.

`-3` shipped a flat first-cut schema (`[default]
repo-remote-provider`, `[github] owner`); `-4` replaces it
with the account/category structure below before init
becomes the first consumer in `-5`. Since `-3` had no
consumers, the rewrite is non-breaking.

Schema:

```toml
[default]
account = "home"      # default --account when absent
debug   = "trace"     # default --debug value when used without arg

[account.home]
repo.default          = "remote"                       # default --repo cat when absent
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:winksaville"   # value for --repo remote (no =val)
repo.category.local   = "~/test-fixtures"              # value for --repo local (no =val)

[account.work]
repo.default          = "remote"
repo.category.remote  = "git@github.com:anthropic"
repo.category.local   = "/work/fixtures"
```

Three layers — each level has its own default-finding key:

| CLI                       | Step 1: account                | Step 2: category                  | Step 3: value                                    |
|---|---|---|---|
| (no `--repo`/`--account`) | `[default].account`            | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--account <a>`           | `<a>` (CLI)                    | `[account.<a>.repo].default`      | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--repo <cat>`            | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `[account.<a>.repo.category].<cat>`              |
| `--repo <cat>=<val>`      | `[default].account` (or CLI)   | `<cat>` (CLI)                     | `<val>` (CLI, literal)                           |

Values are **literal targets**, not section-name
pointers. For `category = "remote"`, the value is a URL
prefix (init appends `/<NAME>.git`); for `category =
"local"`, it's the parent dir for fixture bares. Built-in
categories `remote` and `local` are recognized; any other
category name errors in `-5` (forward-compat: future
cycles can add a `kind` field for user-defined categories).

Resolution errors (each step has its own message):

- Step 1 missing → `no account specified; set [default].account, use --account <name>, or write a top-level [repo] section`.
- Step 2 missing → `no default category for account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo].default or use --repo <cat>`.
- Step 3 missing → `no value for --repo <cat> in account '<a>'; set [account.<a>.repo.category].<cat> or use --repo <cat>=<val>`.

#### Single-account shorthand: top-level `[repo]`

content.content:
998	### Init-lifecycle refactor: prepare/commit split + cross_ref_ochids + init_one elimination (0.41.1-6.5)
999	
1000	Third DRY pass on the init lifecycle. Splits `create_local_repo`
1001	into `prepare_local_repo` + `commit_initial`, lifts step-6
1002	cross-reference rewriting into a named helper, eliminates
1003	`init_one` (inlined into `init_with_symlink`'s POR branch), and
1004	moves role-specific `.vc-config.toml`/`.gitignore` writing out
1005	of the lifecycle primitive into per-role helpers in `init.rs`.
1006	Suite stays at 331, all green.
1007	
1008	**`create_local_repo` → `prepare_local_repo` + `commit_initial`:**
1009	
1010	Splits the former primitive into two sequential operations:
1011	
1012	```
1013	fn prepare_local_repo(target, info_label, template, name) -> Result<()>
1014	fn commit_initial(target, info_label, ochid_strategy)     -> Result<String>
1015	```
1016	
1017	`prepare_local_repo` does mkdir + `git init` + `jj git init
1018	--colocate` + optional template copy + README rewrite. The
1019	working copy is left uncommitted so the caller can drop
1020	role-specific files into the tree. `commit_initial` then runs
1021	`jj commit` (with the appropriate `OchidStrategy` shape) and
1022	returns the new initial commit's chid.
1023	
1024	The split is what makes role-config extraction work: the caller
1025	runs prepare → write_code_config → commit, and the config files
1026	land in the initial commit. (See "Bug caught by the new
1027	substep protocol" below for what happened when the seam
1028	wasn't there.)
1029	
1030	**Role-config helpers (in `init.rs`):**
1031	
1032	```
1033	fn write_por_config(dir)     -> writes VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY + GITIGNORE_APP_ONLY
1034	fn write_code_config(dir)    -> writes VC_CONFIG_CODE     + GITIGNORE_CODE
1035	fn write_session_config(dir) -> writes VC_CONFIG_SESSION  + GITIGNORE_SESSION
1036	```
1037	
1038	Each writes both `.vc-config.toml` and `.gitignore` for its
1039	role. They live in `init.rs` (not `repo_utils.rs`) because the
1040	constants they reference are role-specific to vc-x1's workspace
1041	layout — `repo_utils` stays role-agnostic.
1042	
1043	**`cross_ref_ochids` (in `repo_utils.rs`):**
1044	
1045	```
1046	fn cross_ref_ochids(code_dir, code_chid, session_dir, session_chid) -> Result<()>
1047	```
1048	
1049	Lifted verbatim from `create_dual`'s step-6 region: rewrite both
1050	initial commits' placeholder `ochid: /none` trailers via
1051	`jj describe @-` once each side's chid is known. Pairs with
1052	`OchidStrategy::Placeholder` from `commit_initial` (which writes
1053	the placeholder) — the two together implement the dual-mode
1054	cross-reference dance.
1055	
1056	**`init_one` eliminated:**
1057	
1058	The POR-branch wrapper inlined into `init_with_symlink`. After
1059	the configs/gitignores split out into `write_por_config` and
1060	the lifecycle split into prepare + commit, `init_one`'s body
1061	collapsed to ~25 lines of straight-line composition — no
1062	per-side abstraction left to justify a separate function.
1063	Replaced the dispatch `if is_dual { create_dual } else {
1064	init_one }` with `if is_dual { return create_dual(...); }`
1065	plus the inlined POR body.
1066	
1067	`create_dual` survives as a separate function (its body is
1068	larger and the unwrap-extract-then-orchestrate shape is a
1069	distinct unit).
1070	
1071	**`create_dual` cleanup:**
1072	
1073	Stale "Step N" inline comments dropped — the function names
1074	(`prepare_local_repo`, `cross_ref_ochids`, `push_repo`) now
1075	narrate the lifecycle on their own. Doc comment rewritten as a
1076	bulleted composition list referencing functions, not step
1077	numbers (step numbers shift if the lifecycle reorders, function
1078	names don't).
1079	
1080	The `info!()` lines inside `create_local_repo` and `push_repo`
1081	still carry `"Step N: ..."` labels — those land in -6.7 (single-
1082	word `label: body` convention).
1083	
1084	**Bug caught by the new substep protocol:**
1085	
1086	Substep (1) extracted role-config writing out of
1087	`create_local_repo` into post-call helpers. That left
1088	`.vc-config.toml`/`.gitignore` written *after* `jj commit`
1089	finalized the initial commit — i.e., they sat in the new empty
1090	working copy as uncommitted files. `push_repo`'s subsequent
1091	`git clean -xdf` would have wiped them entirely, and the
1092	`push_happy_claude_clean` integration test caught the related
1093	symptom (`.claude main` advanced because the session side had
1094	pending config files at push time).
1095	
1096	Resolved by substep (5): split the lifecycle (`prepare_local_repo`
1097	+ `commit_initial`) and reorder callers to write configs
1098	between the two. The seam now exists where role-config writing
1099	needs to land — between prepare and commit.
1100	
1101	Lesson logged for the substep protocol: run `cargo test --bins`
1102	after each substep, not only at close-out — would have caught
1103	the regression at (1) instead of (4).
1104	
1105	**Substep protocol — first use:**
1106	
1107	This cycle was the first to use the per-substep `@` workflow:
1108	each substep got its own `jj new` working copy, with the chain
1109	squashed into a single commit at close-out via
1110	`jj squash --from "@---..@" --into @---`. Wins on bisection
1111	(two `jj edit` jumps localized the (1)→(5) regression) and
1112	per-substep diff cleanliness. Frictions: missing
1113	per-substep `cargo test --bins` (above), and squash mechanics
1114	that are worth jotting in CLAUDE.md if the protocol is adopted
1115	formally.
1116	
1117	**WIP ladder:**
1118	
1119	Five substeps, squashed at close-out:
1120	
1121	1. Drop `config`/`gitignore` params from `create_local_repo`;
1122	   add `write_{por,code,session}_config` helpers in `init.rs`.
1123	2. Extract `cross_ref_ochids` into `repo_utils.rs`.
1124	3. Eliminate `init_one` — inline into `init_with_symlink`'s POR
1125	   branch.
1126	4. `create_dual` collapse — drop stale `Step N` comments,
1127	   tighten doc.
1128	5. Fix: split `create_local_repo` into `prepare_local_repo` +
1129	   `commit_initial` (regression from (1) — role-config was
1130	   landing after the initial commit instead of in it).

1135	  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
1136	  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the

file.content:
### Init-lifecycle refactor: prepare/commit split + cross_ref_ochids + init_one elimination (0.41.1-6.5)

Third DRY pass on the init lifecycle. Splits `create_local_repo`
into `prepare_local_repo` + `commit_initial`, lifts step-6
cross-reference rewriting into a named helper, eliminates
`init_one` (inlined into `init_with_symlink`'s POR branch), and
moves role-specific `.vc-config.toml`/`.gitignore` writing out
of the lifecycle primitive into per-role helpers in `init.rs`.
Suite stays at 331, all green.

**`create_local_repo` → `prepare_local_repo` + `commit_initial`:**

Splits the former primitive into two sequential operations:

```
fn prepare_local_repo(target, info_label, template, name) -> Result<()>
fn commit_initial(target, info_label, ochid_strategy)     -> Result<String>
```

`prepare_local_repo` does mkdir + `git init` + `jj git init
--colocate` + optional template copy + README rewrite. The
working copy is left uncommitted so the caller can drop
role-specific files into the tree. `commit_initial` then runs
`jj commit` (with the appropriate `OchidStrategy` shape) and
returns the new initial commit's chid.

The split is what makes role-config extraction work: the caller
runs prepare → write_code_config → commit, and the config files
land in the initial commit. (See "Bug caught by the new
substep protocol" below for what happened when the seam
wasn't there.)

**Role-config helpers (in `init.rs`):**

```
fn write_por_config(dir)     -> writes VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY + GITIGNORE_APP_ONLY
fn write_code_config(dir)    -> writes VC_CONFIG_CODE     + GITIGNORE_CODE
fn write_session_config(dir) -> writes VC_CONFIG_SESSION  + GITIGNORE_SESSION
```

Each writes both `.vc-config.toml` and `.gitignore` for its
role. They live in `init.rs` (not `repo_utils.rs`) because the
constants they reference are role-specific to vc-x1's workspace
layout — `repo_utils` stays role-agnostic.

**`cross_ref_ochids` (in `repo_utils.rs`):**

```
fn cross_ref_ochids(code_dir, code_chid, session_dir, session_chid) -> Result<()>
```

Lifted verbatim from `create_dual`'s step-6 region: rewrite both
initial commits' placeholder `ochid: /none` trailers via
`jj describe @-` once each side's chid is known. Pairs with
`OchidStrategy::Placeholder` from `commit_initial` (which writes
the placeholder) — the two together implement the dual-mode
cross-reference dance.

**`init_one` eliminated:**

The POR-branch wrapper inlined into `init_with_symlink`. After
the configs/gitignores split out into `write_por_config` and
the lifecycle split into prepare + commit, `init_one`'s body
collapsed to ~25 lines of straight-line composition — no
per-side abstraction left to justify a separate function.
Replaced the dispatch `if is_dual { create_dual } else {
init_one }` with `if is_dual { return create_dual(...); }`
plus the inlined POR body.

`create_dual` survives as a separate function (its body is
larger and the unwrap-extract-then-orchestrate shape is a
distinct unit).

**`create_dual` cleanup:**

Stale "Step N" inline comments dropped — the function names
(`prepare_local_repo`, `cross_ref_ochids`, `push_repo`) now
narrate the lifecycle on their own. Doc comment rewritten as a
bulleted composition list referencing functions, not step
numbers (step numbers shift if the lifecycle reorders, function
names don't).

The `info!()` lines inside `create_local_repo` and `push_repo`
still carry `"Step N: ..."` labels — those land in -6.7 (single-
word `label: body` convention).

**Bug caught by the new substep protocol:**

Substep (1) extracted role-config writing out of
`create_local_repo` into post-call helpers. That left
`.vc-config.toml`/`.gitignore` written *after* `jj commit`
finalized the initial commit — i.e., they sat in the new empty
working copy as uncommitted files. `push_repo`'s subsequent
`git clean -xdf` would have wiped them entirely, and the
`push_happy_claude_clean` integration test caught the related
symptom (`.claude main` advanced because the session side had
pending config files at push time).

Resolved by substep (5): split the lifecycle (`prepare_local_repo`
+ `commit_initial`) and reorder callers to write configs
between the two. The seam now exists where role-config writing
needs to land — between prepare and commit.

Lesson logged for the substep protocol: run `cargo test --bins`
after each substep, not only at close-out — would have caught
the regression at (1) instead of (4).

**Substep protocol — first use:**

This cycle was the first to use the per-substep `@` workflow:
each substep got its own `jj new` working copy, with the chain
squashed into a single commit at close-out via
`jj squash --from "@---..@" --into @---`. Wins on bisection
(two `jj edit` jumps localized the (1)→(5) regression) and
per-substep diff cleanliness. Frictions: missing
per-substep `cargo test --bins` (above), and squash mechanics
that are worth jotting in CLAUDE.md if the protocol is adopted
formally.

**WIP ladder:**

Five substeps, squashed at close-out:

1. Drop `config`/`gitignore` params from `create_local_repo`;
   add `write_{por,code,session}_config` helpers in `init.rs`.
2. Extract `cross_ref_ochids` into `repo_utils.rs`.
3. Eliminate `init_one` — inline into `init_with_symlink`'s POR
   branch.
4. `create_dual` collapse — drop stale `Step N` comments,
   tighten doc.
5. Fix: split `create_local_repo` into `prepare_local_repo` +
   `commit_initial` (regression from (1) — role-config was
   landing after the initial commit instead of in it).

### Decisions made during design

- **Version + cycle line.** This work + the sync `--check`
  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the
  in-flight 0.42.0 work on top of both.

615	  gitignore: Option<&str>, template: Option<&Path>, name,
616	  ochid_strategy) -> Result<chid>`. Per-side primitive returning
617	  the new initial commit's chid.
618	- `pub enum OchidStrategy { None, Placeholder }` — initial-commit
619	  message policy. POR uses `None` (plain "Initial commit"); dual
620	  uses `Placeholder` ("Initial commit\n\nochid: /none", rewritten
621	  in step 6 once both chids are known).
622	- 3 unit tests: `strategy_none_writes_plain_commit`,
623	  `strategy_placeholder_writes_ochid_none`,
624	  `no_config_or_gitignore_writes_neither_file`. Last one pins the
625	  new None-skips-write semantics.
626	
627	**Generalizations from -5/-6.1's inline shape:**
628	
629	- `info_label` (was `side_label`) — drops the dual-specific "side"
630	  connotation; the label is just for `info!()` narration. POR
631	  passes `"code"`; dual passes `"code"` then `"bot"`; future
632	  upgrade paths or scratch-repo callers can pass anything else.
633	- `config` and `gitignore` are `Option<&str>` (was `&str`). `None`
634	  skips that file's write. Future use cases:
635	  - PorJjGit / PorWithPeerPor upgrade paths in -6.7/-6.8 leave
636	    existing `.vc-config.toml` in place.
637	  - A hypothetical `vc-x1 scratch-repo` subcommand wanting a bare
638	    git+jj init without project-specific files.
639	
640	**Module rename `repo_url.rs` → `url.rs`:**
641	
642	The old name was misleading — the module's contents (`Target`
643	enum, `parse_target`, `derive_name`, `resolve_url`,
644	`derive_session_url`) parse and derive *URLs and target strings*,
645	not "repos". Pure string manipulation, no repo concept.
646	
647	- File renamed; jj tracks via content.
648	- `repo: &str` parameter on `derive_name` / `resolve_url` →
649	  `url: &str`. Internal references updated to match.
650	- Doc comments tightened: "repo URL" → "URL", "Resolve a repo
651	  argument" → "Resolve a target string", "session repo URL from a
652	  code repo URL" → "session URL from a code-side URL".
653	- All callers updated: `init.rs`, `clone.rs`, `main.rs` (`mod
654	  repo_url;` → `mod url;`).
655	
656	**Renames in `init.rs`:**
657	
658	- `init_dual` → `create_dual`. Symmetric with `create_repo` and
659	  signals that this is a creation-time orchestrator (not a
660	  generic init flow). `init_one` stays — we expect it to collapse
661	  into a 2-call composition by -6.4 (just `create_repo` +
662	  `finalize_repo`) and may not survive as a standalone function.
663	- `init_with_symlink`'s dispatcher updated to call `create_dual`.
664	
665	**Visibility surface (`pub(crate)`) — to support cross-module use
666	from `repo_utils`:**
667	
668	- `jj_chid` (was `fn`).
669	- `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY`, `VC_CONFIG_CODE`, `GITIGNORE_APP_ONLY`,
670	  `GITIGNORE_CODE` (were `const`). Used by repo_utils tests.
671	- `copy_template_recursive`, `rewrite_readme_first_line` were
672	  already `pub(crate)`.
673	
674	**Cosmetic delta in user-visible output:**
675	
676	Dual mode's step narration shifts from one interleaved pass
677	(`Step 1: ...` once, both sides mkdir, `Step 2: ...` once, etc.)
678	to two per-side passes (all five steps for code, then all five
679	for bot). Same actions, same order — just `info!()` ordering.
680	Not covered by tests.
681	
682	**WIP ladder:**
683	
684	Single commit. Bundled the `repo_url` → `url` rename and
685	`init_dual` → `create_dual` rename into the same -6.2 commit
686	since the whole change is "module structure & naming."

file.content:
each subsequent extraction is reviewable against a stable surface.

### Extract create_repo + module reshape (0.41.1-6.2)

First substantive DRY pass on `init_with_symlink`. Lifts steps 1-5
(mkdir, git/jj init, configs, optional template, initial commit)
into a generalized per-side primitive `create_repo` living in a new
`src/repo_utils.rs` module. The dual orchestrator drops from ~180
lines to ~110 by replacing two ~50-line per-side blocks with two
`create_repo` calls. Same suite (320) plus 3 new unit tests for
the helper itself: total 323, all green.

**New module `src/repo_utils.rs`:**

- `pub fn create_repo(target, info_label, config: Option<&str>,
  gitignore: Option<&str>, template: Option<&Path>, name,
  ochid_strategy) -> Result<chid>`. Per-side primitive returning
  the new initial commit's chid.
- `pub enum OchidStrategy { None, Placeholder }` — initial-commit
  message policy. POR uses `None` (plain "Initial commit"); dual
  uses `Placeholder` ("Initial commit\n\nochid: /none", rewritten
  in step 6 once both chids are known).
- 3 unit tests: `strategy_none_writes_plain_commit`,
  `strategy_placeholder_writes_ochid_none`,
  `no_config_or_gitignore_writes_neither_file`. Last one pins the
  new None-skips-write semantics.

**Generalizations from -5/-6.1's inline shape:**

- `info_label` (was `side_label`) — drops the dual-specific "side"
  connotation; the label is just for `info!()` narration. POR
  passes `"code"`; dual passes `"code"` then `"bot"`; future
  upgrade paths or scratch-repo callers can pass anything else.
- `config` and `gitignore` are `Option<&str>` (was `&str`). `None`
  skips that file's write. Future use cases:
  - PorJjGit / PorWithPeerPor upgrade paths in -6.7/-6.8 leave
    existing `.vc-config.toml` in place.
  - A hypothetical `vc-x1 scratch-repo` subcommand wanting a bare
    git+jj init without project-specific files.

**Module rename `repo_url.rs` → `url.rs`:**

The old name was misleading — the module's contents (`Target`
enum, `parse_target`, `derive_name`, `resolve_url`,
`derive_session_url`) parse and derive *URLs and target strings*,
not "repos". Pure string manipulation, no repo concept.

- File renamed; jj tracks via content.
- `repo: &str` parameter on `derive_name` / `resolve_url` →
  `url: &str`. Internal references updated to match.
- Doc comments tightened: "repo URL" → "URL", "Resolve a repo
  argument" → "Resolve a target string", "session repo URL from a
  code repo URL" → "session URL from a code-side URL".
- All callers updated: `init.rs`, `clone.rs`, `main.rs` (`mod
  repo_url;` → `mod url;`).

**Renames in `init.rs`:**

- `init_dual` → `create_dual`. Symmetric with `create_repo` and
  signals that this is a creation-time orchestrator (not a
  generic init flow). `init_one` stays — we expect it to collapse
  into a 2-call composition by -6.4 (just `create_repo` +
  `finalize_repo`) and may not survive as a standalone function.
- `init_with_symlink`'s dispatcher updated to call `create_dual`.

**Visibility surface (`pub(crate)`) — to support cross-module use
from `repo_utils`:**

- `jj_chid` (was `fn`).
- `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY`, `VC_CONFIG_CODE`, `GITIGNORE_APP_ONLY`,
  `GITIGNORE_CODE` (were `const`). Used by repo_utils tests.
- `copy_template_recursive`, `rewrite_readme_first_line` were
  already `pub(crate)`.

**Cosmetic delta in user-visible output:**

Dual mode's step narration shifts from one interleaved pass
(`Step 1: ...` once, both sides mkdir, `Step 2: ...` once, etc.)
to two per-side passes (all five steps for code, then all five
for bot). Same actions, same order — just `info!()` ordering.
Not covered by tests.

**WIP ladder:**

Single commit. Bundled the `repo_url` → `url` rename and
`init_dual` → `create_dual` rename into the same -6.2 commit
since the whole change is "module structure & naming."

### Extract push_repo + rename create_repo → create_local_repo (0.41.1-6.3)

content.text:
`-6.6` is captured as one line in `notes/todo.md:32` and has no design subsection in `chores-08.md` yet. Three concerns in one substep, and the middle one (`symmetric .vc-config.toml schema`) is genuinely ambiguous from the description alone — I'd like your direction before drafting the chores design or touching code.

**Current `.vc-config.toml` schemas** (`src/init.rs:316-360`):

```toml
# POR (single-repo)            # Code side (dual)              # Session side (dual)
[workspace]                    [workspace]                     [workspace]
path = "/"                     path = "/"                      path = "/.claude"
                               other-repo = ".claude"          other-repo = ".."
```

POR is detected by absence of `other-repo`. Reader uses `workspace.path` (find-root walker) and `workspace.other-repo` (`src/common.rs:625-700` area).

**Questions before I draft a -6.6 design subsection:**

1. **`--config=none|<path>` flag (POR).** What's the user-facing intent? `--config=none` skips writing `.vc-config.toml` entirely (so the POR shape becomes "git+jj repo, no vc-x1 marker file at all" — matches the `Other(reason)` state in the init redesign table)? `--config=<path>` writes a custom file or copies one from a path? Or something else?

2. **"Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema (dual)".** Today both dual files have `path` and `other-repo`, but with side-specific values (`"/"` vs `"/.claude"`, `".claude"` vs `".."`). What does "symmetric" mean here — same field names but values differ (already true), or a structural change so both files literally contain the same content (e.g. a `[partner.code]`/`[partner.bot]` two-section schema where each side knows about both)? A worked example of the proposed shape would unblock me.

3. **"Dual-format reader for back-compat".** Reader needs to accept old `[workspace]` schema AND the new symmetric one during a transition window? Detection by which side first — try-new-then-old, or look for a key that disambiguates? Forever or until the next breaking-version bump?

4. **Substep boundaries.** My instinct is a 3-substep ladder: (a) reader change to accept both old + new (back-compat in, no producers yet), (b) new symmetric schema for dual init paths (now using the new reader), (c) `--config=none|<path>` flag for POR. That order means tests stay green at each step — old fixtures still parse, new init writes new shape, then POR gets the new flag. Sound right, or do you want a different breakdown?

Also — the stale `-6.7/-6.8` reference at `chores-08.md:635` (talking about PorJjGit / PorWithPeerPor upgrade paths, which the current `-6.6/-6.7` plan doesn't include) should probably get cleaned up while we're touching the chores file. Flagging.

1219	/// Create-from-empty orchestrator for `--scope=code,bot` (dual).
1220	///
1221	/// Composes (in order):
1222	/// - Code side: `prepare_local_repo` → `write_code_config` →
1223	///   `commit_initial` (`OchidStrategy::Placeholder`).
1224	/// - Session side: `prepare_local_repo` → `write_session_config`
1225	///   → `commit_initial` (`OchidStrategy::Placeholder`).
1226	/// - `cross_ref_ochids` — rewrite both initial commits' placeholder
1227	///   trailers once each side's chid is known.
1228	/// - `push_repo` for session side (no `clean_exclude`).
1229	/// - `push_repo` for code side with `clean_exclude = Some(".claude")`
1230	///   so the nested session repo survives the code-side clean.
1231	/// - `symlink::install` (when `create_symlink`).
1232	fn create_dual(
1233	    args: &InitArgs,
1234	    plan: &InitPlan,
1235	    templates: Option<(PathBuf, Option<PathBuf>)>,
1236	    visibility: &str,
1237	    create_symlink: bool,
1238	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1239	    #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
1240	    let session_dir = plan.session_dir.as_ref().unwrap(); // OK: scope=code,bot ⇒ session_dir set
1241	    #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
1242	    let session_name = plan.session_name.as_ref().unwrap(); // OK: scope=code,bot ⇒ session_name set
1243	    #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
1244	    let session_url = plan.session_url.as_deref().unwrap(); // OK: scope=code,bot ⇒ session_url set
1245	
1246	    let (code_template, bot_template) = match templates.as_ref() {
1247	        Some((c, b)) => (Some(c.as_path()), b.as_deref()),
1248	        None => (None, None),
1249	    };
1250	
1251	    prepare_local_repo(&plan.project_dir, "code", code_template, &plan.name)?;
1252	    write_code_config(&plan.project_dir)?;
1253	    let code_chid = commit_initial(&plan.project_dir, "code", OchidStrategy::Placeholder)?;
1254	
1255	    prepare_local_repo(session_dir, "bot", bot_template, session_name)?;
1256	    write_session_config(session_dir)?;
1257	    let session_chid = commit_initial(session_dir, "bot", OchidStrategy::Placeholder)?;
1258	
1259	    cross_ref_ochids(&plan.project_dir, &code_chid, session_dir, &session_chid)?;
1260	
1261	    let session_chid_final = push_repo(
1262	        session_dir,
1263	        "session",
1264	        "Step 8",
1265	        None,
1266	        plan,
1267	        args,
1268	        visibility,
1269	        session_url,
1270	        plan.gh_session_slug.as_deref(),
1271	        plan.session_bare_path.as_deref(),
1272	    )?;
1273	    // `Some(".claude")` clean_exclude preserves the nested session
1274	    // repo through the code-side `git clean -xdf`.
1275	    let code_chid_final = push_repo(
1276	        &plan.project_dir,
1277	        "code",
1278	        "Step 9",
1279	        Some(".claude"),
1280	        plan,
1281	        args,
1282	        visibility,
1283	        &plan.code_url,
1284	        plan.gh_code_slug.as_deref(),
1285	        plan.code_bare_path.as_deref(),
1286	    )?;
1287	
1288	    let sl_opt = if create_symlink {
1289	        info!("Step 11: Creating Claude Code symlink...");
1290	        Some(symlink::install(&plan.project_dir)?)
1291	    } else {
1292	        info!("Step 11: (skipped — symlink disabled by caller)");
1293	        None
1294	    };
1295	
1296	    info!("");
1297	    info!("Done! Project created at {}", plan.project_dir.display());
1298	    info!(
1299	        "  Code repo:    {}  (chid={code_chid_final})",
1300	        plan.code_url
1301	    );
1302	    info!("  Session repo: {session_url}  (chid={session_chid_final})");
1303	    if let Some(sl) = sl_opt.as_ref() {
1304	        info!(
1305	            "  Symlink:      {} -> {}",
1306	            sl.symlink_path.display(),
1307	            sl.abs_target.display()
1308	        );

1	//! Shared CLI parameter types and value parsers used across
2	//! multiple subcommands.
3	//!
4	//! - `ScopeKind` — typed value of `--scope` for init and clone
5	//!   (and any future subcommand that wants the same
6	//!   `code,bot|por` choice).
7	//! - `parse_scope_kind` — `value_parser` for the `--scope`
8	//!   field; one error wording shared across subcommands.
9	//! - `parse_repo_arg` — `value_parser` for the `--repo
10	//!   <cat>[=<val>]` field; produces `config::RepoSelector`.
11	//!
12	//! Per-subcommand `#[derive(Args)]` structs stay with their
13	//! subcommand because `#[arg(...)]` doc-comments drive
14	//! subcommand-specific `--help` text. Only the cross-cutting
15	//! types and parsers live here.
16	//!
17	//! TODO: the 0.42.0 sum-type cycle (`--scope=...|<path>` everywhere)
18	//! will likely extend `ScopeKind` with `Single(_)` / path-form
19	//! variants and pull more parsers in. See `notes/todo.md` and
20	//! `notes/chores-06.md > Generalize --scope`.
21	
22	use crate::config::RepoSelector;
23	
24	/// Typed value of `--scope` — the kinds of repo set a
25	/// subcommand can target.
26	///
27	/// - `CodeBot` (default) — dual-repo: code + `.claude` bot
28	///   session.
29	/// - `Por` — single repo (Plain Old Repo); no `.claude/`, no
30	///   `.vc-config.toml`.
31	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
32	pub enum ScopeKind {
33	    CodeBot,
34	    Por,
35	}
36	
37	/// Parse the `--scope` value into a `ScopeKind`.
38	///
39	/// - Accepts `code,bot` / `bot,code` (commutative) and `por`.
40	/// - Standalone `code` or `bot` errors — these are
41	///   config-lookup keywords; subcommands using `ScopeKind`
42	///   have no config-driven sides to look up against. Use `por`
43	///   for single-repo or `code,bot` for dual.
44	pub fn parse_scope_kind(s: &str) -> Result<ScopeKind, String> {
45	    match s {
46	        "code,bot" | "bot,code" => Ok(ScopeKind::CodeBot),
47	        "por" => Ok(ScopeKind::Por),
48	        "code" | "bot" => Err(format!(
49	            "'--scope={s}' is not a valid scope kind — use 'code,bot' (dual) or 'por' (single)"
50	        )),
51	        _ => Err(format!(
52	            "'--scope={s}' is not recognized — expected 'code,bot' or 'por'"
53	        )),
54	    }
55	}
56	
57	/// Parse the `--repo` value into a `config::RepoSelector`.
58	///
59	/// - `<cat>` → `RepoSelector { category, value: None }`.
60	/// - `<cat>=<val>` → `RepoSelector { category, value: Some(val) }`.
61	/// - Empty input, empty category, or empty value (after `=`)
62	///   errors.
63	pub fn parse_repo_arg(s: &str) -> Result<RepoSelector, String> {
64	    let s = s.trim();
65	    if s.is_empty() {
66	        return Err("--repo: value is empty".into());
67	    }
68	    match s.split_once('=') {
69	        Some((cat, val)) => {
70	            let cat = cat.trim();
71	            let val = val.trim();
72	            if cat.is_empty() {
73	                return Err(format!("--repo: missing category in '{s}'"));
74	            }
75	            if val.is_empty() {
76	                return Err(format!("--repo: empty value in '{s}'"));
77	            }
78	            Ok(RepoSelector {
79	                category: cat.to_string(),
80	                value: Some(val.to_string()),

content.text:
Got it. Reusable `--config` parser lives in `src/args.rs` next to `ScopeKind`/`parse_scope_kind`. Concrete proposal for `-6.6`:

**Substep ladder (3 substeps, squashed at close-out into one `0.41.1-6.6` commit):**

1. **Extract `create_por()`** — verbatim lift of the inline POR body in `fn init` (lines 1177-1216 of `src/init.rs`) into a new sibling of `create_dual()`. Same signature shape (`args, plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink` — `create_symlink` is a no-op for POR but kept for symmetry, or dropped — see Q below). Dispatcher becomes:
   ```rust
   match args.scope {
       ScopeKind::CodeBot => create_dual(args, &plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink),
       ScopeKind::Por     => create_por(args, &plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink),
   }
   ```
   No behavior change. Doc comment mirrors `create_dual`'s composition list.

2. **Add `ConfigKind` + `parse_config_kind` to `src/args.rs`** — no consumers wired yet. New unit tests in `args.rs` covering each variant + error paths. Mirrors the existing `ScopeKind`/`parse_scope_kind` shape exactly.

3. **Wire `--config` into `InitArgs`; make `write_por_config` conditional in `create_por()`.** Threads the `ConfigKind` through and gates the call. New integration test for each variant.

**Why ladder rather than one commit**: substep 2 lands the reusable type with its own tests in isolation, so the next command that reaches for `--config` (clone, presumably) doesn't have to disentangle init plumbing from the parser. Substep 1 keeps the lift-only diff small and reviewable.

**Open questions before I draft the chores section / start substep 1:**

**Q1 — `ConfigKind` variants and semantics.** My read of `todo.md` is `--config=none|<path>` → 3-state with default:
```rust
pub enum ConfigKind {
    Default,        // no flag → write the canned VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY
    None,           // --config=none → skip write_por_config entirely
    Path(PathBuf),  // --config=<path> → copy <path> to .vc-config.toml
}
```
Or is `Default` not a variant — the `Option<ConfigKind>` at the arg layer carries that and `ConfigKind` only has `None | Path(...)`? `ScopeKind` doesn't have a "Default" variant — the default is encoded by clap's `default_value_t`. I'd lean toward matching that: keep `ConfigKind = None | Path` and let clap's default be "use the canned content" (probably modeled as `Option<ConfigKind>` on the arg, `None` at the arg layer = canned).

Also for `Path(<path>)`: copy the file as-is, or read+validate (parse as TOML, check `[workspace]` shape) before writing? Validate-then-copy is safer; copy-as-is is simpler.

**Q2 — `--config` valid only with `--scope=por`?** Or also meaningful for `--scope=code,bot`? Today `create_dual` writes two role-specific configs unconditionally; the user mentioned only the POR case. Easiest path: error if `--config` is passed with `--scope=code,bot` for now (preflight check in `plan_init`), revisit when dual needs it.

**Q3 — Flag name shape.** `--config <VALUE>` with `none` as a magic keyword and everything else interpreted as a path? Or `--config <none|PATH>` with a custom parser (the proposed shape)? Same model as `--scope=por` — magic keyword + path-form coexist, parser disambiguates.

Once Q1/Q2/Q3 land I'll bump Cargo.toml to `0.41.1-6.6`, draft the chores-08 design subsection, and start substep 1.

Q3: --config <none|Path> but it's optional on the command line so scripting | bots can be explicit and others can be lazy.

Make sense

### Decisions made during design

- **Version + cycle line.** This work + the sync `--check`
  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the
  in-flight 0.42.0 work on top of both.
- **Path-prefix vocabulary.** `./NAME` and the standard
  prefixes (`../`, `/`, `~/`, `~`), plus bare `.` and `..`

### --config option + create_por extraction (0.41.1-6.6)

Pre-impl design. Two concerns in one substep ladder:

1. Extract `create_por()` from `fn init`'s inline POR body
   (currently lines ~1177-1216 of `src/init.rs`), mirroring
   `create_dual()` as a sibling creation-time orchestrator.
2. Add a reusable `--config` flag in `src/args.rs` (next to
   `ScopeKind` / `parse_scope_kind`) that gates POR's
   `.vc-config.toml` write.

#### create_por extraction

`fn init`'s POR branch is currently inline straight-line
composition. Lift verbatim into:

```rust
fn create_por(
    args: &InitArgs,
    plan: &InitPlan,
    templates: Option<(PathBuf, Option<PathBuf>)>,
    visibility: &str,
    _create_symlink: bool,
) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
```

- Signature mirrors `create_dual` for shape-symmetry. The
  `create_symlink` parameter is unused in POR (no symlink in
  single-repo); kept for now so the dispatcher stays trivial.
- Composes (in order, parallel to `create_dual`'s doc list):
  - `prepare_local_repo` → conditional config write (see
    below) → `commit_initial` (`OchidStrategy::None`).
  - `push_repo` for code side (no `clean_exclude`).
  - No cross-reference, no session, no symlink.

Dispatcher in `fn init`:

```rust
if is_dual {
    return create_dual(args, &plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink);
}
return create_por(args, &plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink);
```

#### --config flag (reusable)

In `src/args.rs`:

```rust
pub enum ConfigKind {
    None,
    Path(PathBuf),
}

pub fn parse_config_kind(s: &str) -> Result<ConfigKind, String>;
```

Parser:

- `none` (literal keyword) → `ConfigKind::None`.
- Anything else (non-empty) → `ConfigKind::Path(s.into())`.
- Empty input errors.

No path-prefix discipline (`./`, `~/`, etc.) — `--config` has
only one keyword (`none`), so any other string is unambiguously
a path.

`InitArgs`:

```rust
/// Override the default `.vc-config.toml` write (POR only).
///
/// - Absent: write the canned `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY`.
/// - `--config none`: skip writing.
/// - `--config <path>`: copy the file at `<path>` to
///   `.vc-config.toml`.
#[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
      value_parser = parse_config_kind)]
pub config: Option<ConfigKind>,
```

Optional on the command line so scripts and bots can be
explicit while interactive use stays lazy.

#### Preflight checks (in `plan_init`)

- `args.config.is_some()` with `--scope=code,bot` → error:
  `--config is only valid with --scope=por`.
- `Some(ConfigKind::Path(p))` with non-existent or unreadable
  `p` → error pointing at the path.

#### Conditional write in `create_por`

```rust
match args.config.as_ref() {
    None                       => write_por_config(&plan.project_dir)?,
    Some(ConfigKind::None)     => {} // skip
    Some(ConfigKind::Path(p))  => copy_user_config(p, &plan.project_dir)?,
}
```

`copy_user_config` is a small helper (impl-time call on
location: `init.rs` vs `repo_utils.rs`). Copies as-is; no TOML
parse, no schema validation. Malformed user configs surface
later via `find_workspace_root` / config-reader paths.

Open question for impl: `write_por_config` today writes both
`.vc-config.toml` and `.gitignore`. The `--config` flag should
control only `.vc-config.toml`. Decide between (a) split
`write_por_config` into `write_por_vc_config` +
`write_por_gitignore` and call gitignore unconditionally, or
(b) inline the `.gitignore` write in `create_por` and retire
`write_por_config`. Lean (a) for symmetry with the
`write_code_config` / `write_session_config` shape.

#### WIP ladder (squashed at close-out)

Three substeps:

1. Extract `create_por()` — verbatim lift; no behavior change.
2. Add `ConfigKind` + `parse_config_kind` to `src/args.rs`
   with unit tests; no consumers wired.
3. Wire `--config` into `InitArgs`; conditional write in
   `create_por`; preflight + integration tests.

#### Decisions made during design

- **`Default` not a `ConfigKind` variant.** Absence-of-flag is
  encoded at the `Option<ConfigKind>` arg layer (matches
  `ScopeKind`'s shape — clap's `Option` handles "no value", not
  an enum variant).
- **`--config` only valid with `--scope=por` for now.** Dual
  mode's per-side configs (`write_code_config` /
  `write_session_config`) are out of scope for -6.6; revisit
  when a use case surfaces.
- **Path validation: existence + readable, no TOML parse.**
  Trust user-provided files; downstream readers surface
  malformed content. Preflight stays cheap.
- **`Path(_)` accepts any non-`none` string.** Single keyword,
  no `./` discipline needed.
- **`create_symlink` parameter retained on `create_por`.** Kept
  in the signature for shape-symmetry with `create_dual`;
  ignored in the body. Drop in a follow-up if it accumulates
  warnings.

### Decisions made during design

   `ScopeKind` / `parse_scope_kind`) that gates POR's
   `.vc-config.toml` write.

#### create_por extraction

`fn init`'s POR branch is currently inline straight-line
composition. Lift verbatim into:

```rust
fn create_por(
    args: &InitArgs,
    plan: &InitPlan,
    templates: Option<(PathBuf, Option<PathBuf>)>,
    visibility: &str,
    _create_symlink: bool,
) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
```

- Signature mirrors `create_dual` for shape-symmetry. The
  `create_symlink` parameter is unused in POR (no symlink in
  single-repo); kept for now so the dispatcher stays trivial.

```rust
if is_dual {

}
return create_por(args, &plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink);

pub enum ConfigKind {

input.new_string:
### --config option + create_por extraction (0.41.1-6.6)

Pre-impl design. Two concerns in one substep ladder:

1. Extract `create_por()` from `fn init`'s inline POR body
   (currently lines ~1177-1216 of `src/init.rs`), mirroring
   `create_dual()` as a sibling creation-time orchestrator.
2. Add a reusable `--config` flag in a new module
   `src/options_flags/config.rs` that gates POR's
   `.vc-config.toml` write. New `src/options_flags/` directory
   collects per-flag modules; `src/` is getting large.

#### create_por extraction

`fn init`'s POR branch is currently inline straight-line
composition. Lift verbatim into:

```rust
fn create_por(
    args: &InitArgs,
    plan: &InitPlan,
    templates: Option<(PathBuf, Option<PathBuf>)>,
    visibility: &str,
    _create_symlink: bool,
) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
```

- Signature mirrors `create_dual` for shape-symmetry. The
  `create_symlink` parameter is unused in POR (no symlink in
  single-repo); kept for now so the dispatcher stays trivial.
- Composes (in order, parallel to `create_dual`'s doc list):
  - `prepare_local_repo` → conditional config write (see
    below) → `commit_initial` (`OchidStrategy::None`).
  - `push_repo` for code side (no `clean_exclude`).
  - No cross-reference, no session, no symlink.

Dispatcher in `fn init` — exhaustive `match` on
`args.scope`:

```rust
match args.scope {
    ScopeKind::CodeBot => return create_dual(args, &plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink),
    ScopeKind::Por     => return create_por(args, &plan, templates, visibility, create_symlink),
}
```

`is_dual` (computed earlier in `fn init` for the dry-run/print
branch) stays in place for that branch; only the final dispatch
line changes from the existing `if is_dual { … }` to the
`match`. Exhaustive `match` means future `ScopeKind` variants
won't compile until handled here.

#### --config flag (reusable)

New module `src/options_flags/config.rs`:

```rust
pub enum ConfigKind {
    None,
    Path(PathBuf),
}

pub fn parse_config_kind(s: &str) -> Result<ConfigKind, String>;
```

`src/options_flags/mod.rs` re-exports public items (`pub mod
config; pub use config::{ConfigKind, parse_config_kind};` — or
require `crate::options_flags::config::ConfigKind` at use
sites; pick at impl time based on import noise).

`src/main.rs` adds `mod options_flags;` alongside the existing
`mod args;`.

**Changes:**
- New `src/options_flags/mod.rs` — directory module docstring + `pub mod config;`.

content.text:
I'm not sure I'm reading the intent right — `parse_config_kind` as a clap `value_parser` is called with just `s: &str` (clap doesn't pass extra args). A few plausible reshapes; want to land on the right one before editing:

**Option A — parser takes a default, used for empty input:**
```rust
pub fn parse_config_kind(s: &str, default: ConfigKind) -> Result<ConfigKind, String> {
    Ok(match s {
        ""     => default,
        "none" => ConfigKind::None,
        _      => ConfigKind::Path(s.into()),
    })
}
```
Caller (init) calls with its canned default, e.g. `parse_config_kind(s, ConfigKind::Path(canned_vc_config_path))`. Doesn't fit clap's `value_parser` slot — would have to be wrapped (e.g. `value_parser = |s| parse_config_kind(s, init_default())`) or the parsing moves out of clap into the consumer.

**Option B — factory that closes over the default:**
```rust
pub fn make_parser(default: ConfigKind) -> impl Fn(&str) -> Result<ConfigKind, String> { ... }
```
Used as `value_parser = make_parser(ConfigKind::Default)` — clap-friendly. But this still implies a "Default" variant or a sentinel value to embed.

**Option C — add a `Default` variant after all** (revisits Q1):
```rust
pub enum ConfigKind { Default, None, Path(PathBuf) }
```
Parser matches `default | none | <path>`. Consumer's match exhausts all three.

**Option D — something else entirely.** Maybe you mean the parser body should restructure (just match-based, not the cascading if-statements I wrote) without any signature change? That'd be:
```rust
pub fn parse_config_kind(s: &str) -> Result<ConfigKind, String> {
    Ok(match s {
        ""     => return Err("--config: value is empty (use 'none' or a path)".into()),
        "none" => ConfigKind::None,
        _      => ConfigKind::Path(s.into()),
    })
}
```
Pure stylistic refactor, no semantic change.

Which one — and where does the "default" live (caller-supplied, embedded sentinel, or new enum variant)?

input.new_string:
New module `src/options_flags/config.rs`:

```rust
pub enum ConfigKind {
    None,
    Path(PathBuf),
}

pub fn parse_config_kind(s: &str, default: ConfigKind) -> ConfigKind;
```

Parser (match-based):

- `""` → `default` (caller-supplied).
- `"none"` → `ConfigKind::None`.
- Anything else → `ConfigKind::Path(s.into())`.

Each consumer plugs its own canonical canned shape into
`default`; init's POR uses `ConfigKind::Path(<canned VC config
file>)` (or a marker the consumer interprets — see substep 3).
This puts the per-command default *at the call site* rather
than baking a `Default` variant into the enum.

No path-prefix discipline (`./`, `~/`, etc.) — `--config` has
only one keyword (`none`), so any other string is unambiguously
a path. The parser is infallible — no `Result` return — because
empty input has a defined fallback.

file.content:
#### --config flag (reusable)

New module `src/options_flags/config.rs`:

```rust
pub enum ConfigKind {
    None,
    Path(PathBuf),
}

pub fn parse_config_kind(s: &str) -> Result<ConfigKind, String>;
```

`src/options_flags/mod.rs` re-exports public items (`pub mod
config; pub use config::{ConfigKind, parse_config_kind};` — or
require `crate::options_flags::config::ConfigKind` at use
sites; pick at impl time based on import noise).

`src/main.rs` adds `mod options_flags;` alongside the existing
`mod args;`.

Parser:

- `none` (literal keyword) → `ConfigKind::None`.
- Anything else (non-empty) → `ConfigKind::Path(s.into())`.
- Empty input errors.

No path-prefix discipline (`./`, `~/`, etc.) — `--config` has
only one keyword (`none`), so any other string is unambiguously
a path.

`InitArgs`:

```rust
/// Override the default `.vc-config.toml` write (POR only).
///
/// - Absent: write the canned `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY`.
/// - `--config none`: skip writing.
/// - `--config <path>`: copy the file at `<path>` to
///   `.vc-config.toml`.
#[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
      value_parser = parse_config_kind)]
pub config: Option<ConfigKind>,
```

Optional on the command line so scripts and bots can be
explicit while interactive use stays lazy.

#### Preflight checks (in `plan_init`)

input.new_string:
```rust
pub enum ConfigKind {
    None,
    Path(PathBuf),
}

pub fn parse_config_kind(s: &str, default: ConfigKind) -> ConfigKind;
```

`src/options_flags/mod.rs` only re-exports the submodule (`pub
mod config;`); use sites import `crate::options_flags::config::
{ConfigKind, parse_config_kind}` so future flag modules can
share the namespace without name collisions.

`src/main.rs` adds `mod options_flags;` alongside the existing
`mod args;`.

Parser (match-based, infallible):

- `""` → `default` (caller-supplied — each consumer plugs in
  its own canonical canned shape).
- `"none"` → `ConfigKind::None`.
- Anything else → `ConfigKind::Path(s.into())`.

The default lives at the call site, not in the enum. Init's POR
passes a default that resolves to "write the canned
`VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY`" (substep-3 wiring decides the exact
shape — likely a marker variant the consumer interprets, or a
synthetic `Path` to a temp file).

No path-prefix discipline (`./`, `~/`, etc.) — `--config` has
only one keyword (`none`), so any other string is unambiguously
a path. Parser returns `ConfigKind` directly (no `Result`)
because empty input has a defined fallback rather than an error.

`write_por_gitignore` and call gitignore unconditionally, or
(b) inline the `.gitignore` write in `create_por` and retire
`write_por_config`. Lean (a) for symmetry with the
`write_code_config` / `write_session_config` shape.

3. Wire `--config` into `InitArgs`; conditional write in
   `create_por`; preflight + integration tests.

#### Decisions made during design

- **`Default` not a `ConfigKind` variant.** Absence-of-flag is
  encoded at the `Option<ConfigKind>` arg layer (matches
  `ScopeKind`'s shape — clap's `Option` handles "no value", not
  an enum variant).
- **`--config` only valid with `--scope=por` for now.** Dual
  mode's per-side configs (`write_code_config` /
  `write_session_config`) are out of scope for -6.6; revisit
  when a use case surfaces.
- **Path validation: existence + readable, no TOML parse.**
  Trust user-provided files; downstream readers surface
  malformed content. Preflight stays cheap.
- **`Path(_)` accepts any non-`none` string.** Single keyword,
  no `./` discipline needed.
- **`create_symlink` parameter retained on `create_por`.** Kept
  in the signature for shape-symmetry with `create_dual`;
  ignored in the body. Drop in a follow-up if it accumulates
  warnings.
- **Dispatcher uses exhaustive `match` on `args.scope`.** Future
  `ScopeKind` variants force a compile error here until handled
  — preferable to `if is_dual { … } else { … }`, which silently
  routes any new variant to the POR arm.
- **`src/options_flags/` directory.** New home for per-flag
  modules as `src/` grows. `--config` lands here first; whether
  to relocate the existing `ScopeKind` / `parse_scope_kind`
  from `src/args.rs` to `src/options_flags/scope.rs` is an
  open follow-up (not in -6.6).

### Decisions made during design

- **Version + cycle line.** This work + the sync `--check`
  fix land on the 0.41.x line. Init+clone = 0.41.1. Sync
  fix = a separate cycle (likely 0.41.2). Then rebase the
  in-flight 0.42.0 work on top of both.
- **Path-prefix vocabulary.** `./NAME` and the standard
  prefixes (`../`, `/`, `~/`, `~`), plus bare `.` and `..`
  (POSIX cwd / parent — unambiguous). Bare alphanumeric
  `NAME` is an error — explicit prefix required.
- **`--private` on existing remote.** Warn and ignore;
  visibility was set at create time.
- **Cosmetic anomalies** from `notes/vc-x1-init.md` —

  `Result` because every input shape is a definite parse —

1	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
2	
3	use clap::Args;
4	use log::{debug, info};
5	
6	use crate::args::{ScopeKind, parse_repo_arg, parse_scope_kind};
7	use crate::config::{self, RepoSelector, UserConfig};
8	use crate::repo_utils::{OchidStrategy, commit_initial, cross_ref_ochids, prepare_local_repo};
9	use crate::scope::{Scope, Side};
10	use crate::symlink;
11	use crate::url::{Target, derive_name, derive_session_url, parse_target};
12	
13	/// CLI args for `vc-x1 init`.
14	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
15	pub struct InitArgs {
16	    /// Target — URL, owner/name shorthand, path, or bare NAME.
17	    ///
18	    /// - URL: `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
19	    ///   — used as-is; config not consulted.
20	    /// - owner/name shorthand: resolves to
21	    ///   `git@github.com:owner/name.git`; config not consulted.

content.content:
1247	#### Preflight checks (in `plan_init`)
1248	
1249	- `args.config.is_some()` with `--scope=code,bot` → error:
1250	  `--config is only valid with --scope=por`.
1251	- `parse_config_kind(...) → Path(p)` with non-existent or
1252	  unreadable `p` → error pointing at the path.
1253	
1254	#### Conditional write in `create_por`
1255	
1256	```rust
1257	let config_kind = parse_config_kind(
1258	    args.config.as_deref().unwrap_or(""),
1259	    /* default */ ConfigKind::Path(canned_path),  // shape TBD substep 3
1260	);
1261	match config_kind {
1262	    ConfigKind::None    => {} // skip — user asked us not to write
1263	    ConfigKind::Path(p) => copy_user_config(&p, &plan.project_dir)?,
1264	}
1265	```
1266	
1267	Sketch shape — substep 3 picks the actual default representation
1268	for "write the canned content". Two candidates: a synthetic
1269	`Path` to a tempfile with the canned bytes, or interpret a
1270	specific path as "use canned" sentinel (less clean).
1271	
1272	`copy_user_config` is a small helper (impl-time call on
1273	location: `init.rs` vs `repo_utils.rs`). Copies as-is; no TOML
1274	parse, no schema validation. Malformed user configs surface
1275	later via `find_workspace_root` / config-reader paths.
1276	
1277	Open question for impl: `write_por_config` today writes both
1278	`.vc-config.toml` and `.gitignore`. The `--config` flag should
1279	control only `.vc-config.toml`. Decide between (a) split
1280	`write_por_config` into `write_por_vc_config` +
1281	`write_por_gitignore` and call gitignore unconditionally, or
1282	(b) inline the `.gitignore` write in `create_por` and retire
1283	`write_por_config`. Lean (a) for symmetry with the
1284	`write_code_config` / `write_session_config` shape.
1285	
1286	#### WIP ladder (squashed at close-out)
1287

#### Preflight checks (in `plan_init`)

- `args.config.is_some()` with `--scope=code,bot` → error:
  `--config is only valid with --scope=por`.
- `parse_config_kind(...) → Path(p)` with non-existent or
  unreadable `p` → error pointing at the path.

#### Conditional write in `create_por`

```rust
let config_kind = parse_config_kind(
    args.config.as_deref().unwrap_or(""),
    /* default */ ConfigKind::Path(canned_path),  // shape TBD substep 3
);
match config_kind {
    ConfigKind::None    => {} // skip — user asked us not to write
    ConfigKind::Path(p) => copy_user_config(&p, &plan.project_dir)?,
}
```

Sketch shape — substep 3 picks the actual default representation
for "write the canned content". Two candidates: a synthetic
`Path` to a tempfile with the canned bytes, or interpret a
specific path as "use canned" sentinel (less clean).

`copy_user_config` is a small helper (impl-time call on
location: `init.rs` vs `repo_utils.rs`). Copies as-is; no TOML
parse, no schema validation. Malformed user configs surface
later via `find_workspace_root` / config-reader paths.

Open question for impl: `write_por_config` today writes both
`.vc-config.toml` and `.gitignore`. The `--config` flag should
control only `.vc-config.toml`. Decide between (a) split
`write_por_config` into `write_por_vc_config` +
`write_por_gitignore` and call gitignore unconditionally, or
(b) inline the `.gitignore` write in `create_por` and retire
`write_por_config`. Lean (a) for symmetry with the
`write_code_config` / `write_session_config` shape.

#### WIP ladder (squashed at close-out)

Three substeps:

1. Extract `create_por()` and convert dispatcher to `match` —
   verbatim lift; no behavior change.

input.new_string:
#### Preflight checks (in `plan_init`) - `args.config.is_some()` with `--scope=code,bot` → error: `--config is only valid with --scope=por (dual-mode configs are per-side and unconditional)`. - `parse_config_kind(s, ConfigKind::None) → Path(p)` where `p` doesn't exist → error: `--config: path does not exist:

`. (Existence check only — readability surfaces later as a copy-time error with a clear `failed to copy ` message.) #### Conditional write in `create_por` ```rust match args.config.as_deref() { None => write_por_vc_config(&plan.project_dir)?, Some(s) => match parse_config_kind(s, ConfigKind::None) { ConfigKind::None => {} // skip — user asked us not to write ConfigKind::Path(p) => copy_user_config(&p, &plan.project_dir)?, }, } write_por_gitignore(&plan.project_dir)?; ``` Resolved at impl time (substep 3): - **No tempfile / sentinel for the canned default.** The consumer dispatches on `args.config.as_deref()` directly: `None` → canned write, `Some(s)` → parser. The parser's `default` parameter is passed `ConfigKind::None` and is only reachable if the user passes `--config ""` explicitly (in which case "skip" is a reasonable interpretation). This avoids both the tempfile dance and the sentinel-Path hack. - **`write_por_config` split (option (a) from the open question).** New helpers `write_por_vc_config` (canned config) and `write_por_gitignore` (canned gitignore). The latter is called unconditionally after the conditional config write. Symmetric with the existing `write_code_config` / `write_session_config` shape. - **`copy_user_config` lives in `init.rs`.** Sits next to `write_por_vc_config` / `write_por_gitignore`. Bytewise `std::fs::copy` (no TOML parse, no validation). Malformed user content surfaces later via `find_workspace_root` / config readers. No new module needed.

content.text:
Substep 3 complete. **343 tests pass** (was 338; +5 new for `--config` integration).

**Changes:**

`src/init.rs`:
- Imports `ConfigKind` + `parse_config_kind` from `options_flags::config`.
- `InitArgs.config: Option<String>` (raw at arg layer; consumer-side parse).
- New helpers: `write_por_vc_config`, `write_por_gitignore` (split from `write_por_config`), `copy_user_config` (bytewise `std::fs::copy`).
- `plan_init` preflight: rejects `--config` with `--scope=code,bot`; validates path existence for `Some(s) → Path(p)`.
- `create_por` dispatches on `args.config.as_deref()`: `None` → canned write, `Some(s)` → `parse_config_kind(s, ConfigKind::None)` → match. Gitignore always written.
- 5 new tests at the end of the POR fixture block.

`src/test_helpers.rs`:
- `FixturePor::new_with_config(tag, config)` constructor; `new(tag)` delegates to it with `None`.

`src/options_flags/config.rs`:
- Dropped `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `ConfigKind` and `parse_config_kind` — they're now consumed.

`notes/chores-08.md`:
- Replaced "open question" + sketch code blocks with the actual shipped impl. Captured three resolved decisions: no tempfile/sentinel needed, `write_por_config` split (option (a)), `copy_user_config` lives in `init.rs`.

**Verification:** `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --bins --tests -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --bins`, `cargo install --path . --locked`. `vc-x1 init --help` rendered the `--config <none|PATH>` block correctly.

Stopping here. Next step would be close-out: squash the three substeps via `jj squash --from "<base>..@-" --into @ -u -R .` (or however the protocol prescribes for ladder-of-3), then prepare the commit message and run `vc-x1 push main`. Awaiting your signal.

message.content: Seems to me each subcommand should know what flags and options they handle, that way a particular subcommands parameters can change, thoughts

content.text:
Agreed — and that observation pushes against my earlier "reusable" framing. `ConfigKind` is used only by init right now; placing it in `src/options_flags/` was speculative sharing, not justified by a second consumer. The principle "share when there's a real second consumer, otherwise own" applies cleanly here: `ScopeKind` in `src/args.rs` survives because init *and* clone both use it; `ConfigKind` doesn't have that justification yet.

**Recommendation:** co-locate `ConfigKind` + `parse_config_kind` with `InitArgs` (either inside `src/init.rs` next to the struct, or a new `src/init/` directory if we want to start a per-subcommand-as-directory pattern), and drop `src/options_flags/`. When clone (or another command) actually needs `--config`, lift to shared then — informed by two real use cases instead of one speculative one.

**Tradeoff:** lose the "documentation by directory structure" signal (placing it in `options_flags/` advertises intent to share) in exchange for cleaner subcommand boundaries and no premature coupling. The shared-module path is also still open as a follow-up if/when justified — moving things into shared modules later is mechanical.

**Timing question:** fold the refactor into -6.6 by rewriting substep 2 (and the import change in substep 3), or ship -6.6 as-is and do a follow-up cycle? My lean is fold-now — the substep ladder hasn't squashed yet, the diff is small (move one file's contents, adjust one import), and the close-out commit reads cleaner without a "we put it in options_flags/ then immediately moved it back" history.

Want me to redo substep 2 (move `ConfigKind`/`parse_config_kind` into `src/init.rs`; drop `src/options_flags/`; adjust substep 3's import; chores rewrite the `options_flags/` decision)?

content.text:
Got it — `options_flags/` as the default home for all flag types/parsers, regardless of current consumer count, with cross-project reusability as a forward-looking goal. That makes the existing chores decision (the one calling `options_flags/` "the new home for per-flag modules") accurate as-is, and reframes the "relocate `ScopeKind`" follow-up as a consistency cleanup rather than a maybe.

One small wording tweak worth offering: the chores decision currently reads as a neutral "where things land," but doesn't articulate the principle (default = `options_flags/`, even for single-consumer flags, with cross-project reuse as a future). Want me to strengthen the wording — e.g.:

> **`src/options_flags/` directory — default home for all flag types/parsers.** Each flag's typed value + value parser lives here, regardless of how many subcommands consume it today. Two reasons: (1) any flag may pick up additional consumers as the CLI grows, and (2) the directory is shaped to be liftable into its own crate for cross-project reuse later. `ScopeKind` / `parse_scope_kind` currently lives in `src/args.rs` for historical reasons; relocating to `src/options_flags/scope.rs` is a deferred consistency cleanup (not in -6.6).

Otherwise the design captures it well enough. Either way, I think substep 3 stands as shipped — ready for close-out (squash the three substeps + push) on your signal.

message.content: init_with_simlink is a terrible name and needs to be refactored, I think it should go with the scope change and we have create_dual handle its options and create_por handle it's options, but obviouslyl almost all of the options/flags are supported in both except for config so they all should go in option_flags share a ton of code. share a ton of code. init_with_symlink is a terrible name and needs to be refactored, I think it should go with the scope change and we have create_dual handle its options and create_por handle it's options, but obviouslyl almost all of the options/flags are supported in both except for config so they all should go in option_flags share a ton of code. share a ton of code. content.text:
Name agreed — `init_with_symlink` is misleading; the symlink is incidental and the `create_symlink: bool` is false half the time. Code sharing across `create_dual` / `create_por` is real (both pipelines are `prepare → write_config → commit → push`), and a helper like `provision_side(role, …) -> chid` would shrink both to thin orchestrators.

Two ambiguities to resolve before concrete edits:

**Q1 — Args struct shape.** "create_dual handles its options" could mean either:
- (a) Keep one `InitArgs`; just relabel and rely on preflight to validate scope-specific fields (today's shape with `--config` preflight-gated).
- (b) Split into `InitDualArgs` + `InitPorArgs`, both embedding a common `InitCommonArgs` via clap's `#[command(flatten)]`. Type system enforces "no `--config` with dual"; adds three structs.

(b) tracks your "create_X handles its own options" framing more literally, but pushes us into clap subcommand territory (`vc-x1 init dual ...` vs `vc-x1 init por ...`) — or a manual two-pass parser.

**Q2 — What "all in options_flags" means.** Either:
- (a) Per-flag value+parser modules (today's pattern: one file per flag like `config.rs`). The cross-cutting struct (`InitCommonArgs` if we go (1b)) lives... where? Init-side? Or also `options_flags/`?
- (b) `options_flags/` also hosts shared option-bundle structs (e.g. `options_flags/init_common.rs` holding `InitCommonArgs`), so cross-project reuse picks up groups of related flags, not just individual ones.

**Timing recommendation:** defer to its own substep — `-6.8` (after `-6.7`'s log-label work) feels right. -6.6 has shipped a focused unit ("config flag + create_por extraction"); mixing in a rename + Args reshape would muddy the squash and make the diff harder to review. Once Q1/Q2 land we can scope -6.8 cleanly.

Which interpretations do you have in mind?

lastPrompt: init_with_symlink is a terrible name and needs to be refactored, I think it should go with the scope change and we have create_dual handle its options and create_por handle it's options, but obviously…

message.content: Q1 (b), Q2 flags should be individual but there should be a technique for bundling common sets, -6.8 is fine

content.text:
Confirmed. -6.8 covers: `init_with_symlink` rename, `InitDualArgs` + `InitPorArgs` (each flatten a common bundle, type-enforces "no `--config` with dual"), individual flag modules in `options_flags/` plus a bundling technique for common sets, and shared `provision_side(role, …)` helper for the dual/por pipelines.

**One residual sub-question for -6.8 (no decision needed now, just surfacing):** clap-native subcommands `vc-x1 init dual …` / `vc-x1 init por …` (breaks current `--scope=por` UX, but matches Q1(b)'s shape natively), or preserve `--scope` as a flag via manual two-pass parsing (parse `--scope` first, then re-parse against the matching Args struct)? Subcommands are clap-cleaner and surface the choice in `--help`; the manual approach keeps backward compat but adds parsing machinery. Lean subcommands since 0.41.x is pre-1.0, but the call is yours at -6.8 design time.

**Bundling technique sketch (for -6.8 design):** a `options_flags/bundles/init_common.rs` (or `options_flags/init_common_args.rs`) holding the cross-cutting `InitCommonArgs` struct that flattens the individual flag-type structs from sibling modules. Keeps per-flag modules pure (one type per file) while giving consumers a one-import-then-flatten group reference.

**Now:** want me to (a) add a `-6.8` entry to the `notes/todo.md` ladder with a one-liner pointing forward, then (b) proceed to -6.6 close-out (squash substeps 1–3 + run the push flow)? Or pause for you to think on the CLI surface question first?

**Close-out squashed cleanly.** Single commit at `@` (`vnqvplvp 316f016b`) on top of `init-clone-refactor` (`sumruqqo a8c1eefe`). 9 files, 515 insertions / 16 deletions. **343 tests pass.**

**Consolidated diff (close-out):**
- `Cargo.toml` + `Cargo.lock` — version bump 0.41.1-6.5 → 0.41.1-6.6.
- `notes/todo.md` — `-6.6` marked done with revised scope; `-6.8` entry added with the trait-bundle sketch.
- `notes/chores-08.md` — new `### --config option + create_por extraction (0.41.1-6.6)` design subsection (post-impl).
- `src/init.rs` — `create_por` extracted; dispatcher uses `match args.scope`; `write_por_config` split into `write_por_vc_config` + `write_por_gitignore`; new `copy_user_config`; `InitArgs.config: Option<String>`; preflight in `plan_init` (scope/path checks); 5 new tests.
- `src/options_flags/mod.rs` + `src/options_flags/config.rs` — new directory; `ConfigKind { None, Path(PathBuf) }` + `parse_config_kind(s, default) -> ConfigKind` (Option A: caller-supplied default for empty input, infallible).
- `src/main.rs` — `mod options_flags;` added.
- `src/test_helpers.rs` — `FixturePor::new_with_config(tag, config)` constructor.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

message.content: Let's commit, push and finalize to the repo

content.text:
Proposed commit message for the close-out push:

**Title** (43 chars):
```
feat: init --config flag (POR) (0.41.1-6.6)
```

**Body**:
```
Lifts `fn init`'s inline POR body into a sibling `create_por`
matching `create_dual`'s shape; dispatcher converted to
exhaustive `match args.scope`. Adds POR-only `--config
none|<path>` flag gating `.vc-config.toml` writing (no flag =
canned, `none` = skip, `<path>` = copy user file). The flag's
typed value + parser live in a new `src/options_flags/`
directory — default home for all flag types/parsers going
forward.

- Cargo.toml: 0.41.1-6.5 → 0.41.1-6.6
- src/init.rs: create_por extraction; match dispatcher; split
  write_por_config; new copy_user_config; InitArgs.config;
  preflight + 5 new tests
- src/options_flags/{mod,config}.rs: new directory; ConfigKind
  + parse_config_kind (Option A: caller default, infallible)
  + 7 unit tests
- src/main.rs: mod options_flags;
- src/test_helpers.rs: FixturePor::new_with_config
- notes/chores-08.md: -6.6 design subsection
- notes/todo.md: -6.6 done; -6.8 added; In Progress ladder
  wrapped <80 cols
```

Bookmark target: `init-clone-refactor`. May I run `vc-x1 push init-clone-refactor` with the above title/body?

lastPrompt: Let's commit, push and finalize to the repo

Lifts `fn init`'s inline POR body into a sibling `create_por`
matching `create_dual`'s shape; dispatcher converted to
exhaustive `match args.scope`. Adds POR-only `--config
none|<path>` flag gating `.vc-config.toml` writing (no flag =
canned, `none` = skip, `<path>` = copy user file). The flag's
typed value + parser live in a new `src/options_flags/`
directory — default home for all flag types/parsers going
forward.

- Cargo.toml: 0.41.1-6.5 → 0.41.1-6.6
- src/init.rs: create_por extraction; match dispatcher; split
  write_por_config; new copy_user_config; InitArgs.config;
  preflight + 5 new tests
- src/options_flags/{mod,config}.rs: new directory; ConfigKind
  + parse_config_kind (Option A: caller default, infallible)
  + 7 unit tests
- src/main.rs: mod options_flags;
- src/test_helpers.rs: FixturePor::new_with_config
- notes/chores-08.md: -6.6 design subsection
- notes/todo.md: -6.6 done; -6.8 added; In Progress ladder
  wrapped <80 cols
EOF
)"
